statute_links.description: Federal law providing 6% interest cap on pre-service debts, foreclosure/eviction protections, and contract termination rights for active-duty service members.

FL.recent_changes: No significant changes to Florida's core consumer lending or credit repair legislation in 2024-2025. The payday loan regulatory framework, usury caps, and credit repair requirements remain substantially unchanged. However, continued federal enforcement of the Military Lending Act and SCRA in Florida, particularly targeting non-compliance at payday lenders near military bases, reflects ongoing regulatory focus.

GA.name: Georgia
GA.usury_cap: 5% simple interest (7% contract rate); payday lending banned under industrial loan act repeal
GA.consumer_protection_agency: Georgia Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
GA.licensing_board: Georgia Department of Banking and Finance
key_regulations: Payday lending is banned; Georgia repealed the industrial loan act that authorized small loans.
The Georgia Industrial Loan Act criminalizes payday-style lending as a felony racketeering offense.
Licensed installment lenders can charge tiered rates up to 60% for smallest loans.
Georgia Fair Lending Act regulates high-cost home loans.
GA.consumer_rights_summary: Georgia bans payday lending and treats violations as felony racketeering, providing among the strongest anti-payday protections in the country. Licensed installment lenders are regulated by the Department of Banking and Finance. Consumers can file complaints through the Governor's Office of Consumer Protection.

credit_repair_laws.state_statute: Georgia Credit Repair Services Organization Statute (criminal)
key_provisions: Credit repair organizations must provide written contract with clear terms, cost, and timeline before providing services
Upfront payment for credit repair services is prohibited; payment only allowed after promised results are delivered
Organizations must disclose consumer's right to dispute items directly with credit bureaus at no cost
Credit repair services cannot charge fees for disputing items on credit reports or contacting creditors
Contracts must include cancellation clause allowing consumers to cancel within 5 business days with full refund
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: Georgia Attorney General Consumer Protection Division

personal_loans.rate_cap: 5% simple interest (7% contract rate) under usury laws; no specific cap for licensed lenders making installment loans
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans from licensed lenders are governed by Georgia Consumer Finance Law. Unlicensed lenders are subject to usury caps.

payday_loans.rate_cap: Banned
payday_loans.notes: Georgia repealed the Industrial Loan Act and criminalizes payday-style lending as a felony racketeering offense under Ga. Code Ann. § 16-8-1 et seq. Violation can result in criminal prosecution.

title_loans.rate_cap: Effectively banned through payday lending prohibition
title_loans.notes: Title loans are not explicitly licensed or regulated in Georgia and fall under payday lending restrictions; illegal operators subject to felony charges.

installment_loans.rate_cap: Licensed installment lenders can charge tiered rates up to 60% APR on smallest loans, declining with loan size; rates regulated by Georgia Consumer Finance Law (Ga. Code Ann. § 34-29-1 et seq.)
installment_loans.notes: Licensed by Georgia Department of Banking and Finance. Rates vary by loan amount with maximum rates decreasing as loan amounts increase. Lenders must be licensed and comply with all regulatory requirements.

mortgage.notes: Georgia is a non-judicial foreclosure state. Mortgages regulated under Ga. Code Ann. § 34-29-1 et seq. and foreclosure rules under Ga. Code Ann. § 44-14-1 et seq. Georgia Fair Lending Act (Ga. Code Ann. § 34-60-1 et seq.) regulates high-cost home loans and requires certain disclosures for loans with APR over 8% or fees/points exceeding 5% of loan amount.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 987) applies to active-duty service members and their dependents in Georgia. Creditors are prohibited from charging an annual percentage rate (MAPR) exceeding 36% on covered borrowing, and cannot require mandatory arbitration or waivers of legal rights. Georgia has no state-level override of federal MLA protections.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3901 et seq.) applies to active-duty service members. SCRA provides a 6% interest rate cap on pre-service debts, foreclosure/repossession protections, lease termination rights, and stay of eviction proceedings. Service members must provide notice of active duty status to claim benefits.
state_specific_protections: Georgia provides property tax exemptions for disabled veterans and their surviving spouses under Ga. Code Ann. § 49-5-4
Georgia Department of Veterans Services offers benefits counseling and assistance programs to eligible veterans
Georgia housing authority programs and state-level VA loan support available through Georgia Housing and Finance Authority
Discrimination against service members in lending is prohibited under both federal and Georgia consumer protection laws
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: Georgia Housing and Finance Authority (GHFA) administers VA loan programs and conventional mortgage programs for veterans. Veterans may be eligible for state-specific benefits including property tax exemptions for 100% disabled veterans and certain surviving spouses.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Major military installations in Georgia include Fort Benning (Columbus), Fort Gordon (Augusta), Fort Stewart (Hinesville), Moody Air Force Base (Valdosta), and Kings Bay Naval Submarine Base (St. Marys). These areas are priorities for Military Lending Act enforcement by federal regulators.

complaint_resources.name: Georgia Department of Banking and Finance
complaint_resources.description: Handles complaints against licensed lenders, credit repair organizations, and other financial institutions. Oversees compliance with Georgia lending and credit laws.

complaint_resources.name: Georgia Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
complaint_resources.description: Investigates and prosecutes violations of Georgia consumer protection laws, including payday lending violations, credit repair fraud, and lending discrimination. Accepts consumer complaints.

complaint_resources.name: Governor's Office of Consumer Protection
complaint_resources.description: Assists consumers with complaints regarding unfair lending practices, credit repair fraud, and other consumer finance issues.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Federal agency handling complaints about banks, credit unions, payday lenders, credit reporting agencies, debt collectors, and other consumer finance companies for violations of federal law.

complaint_resources.name: Better Business Bureau - Georgia
complaint_resources.description: Provides dispute resolution services and maintains complaint records for businesses, including lenders and credit repair companies.

statute_links.name: Georgia Consumer Finance Law
statute_links.description: Ga. Code Ann. § 34-29-1 et seq. - Governs installment loans, rates, fees, and licensing of consumer finance lenders.

statute_links.name: Georgia Industrial Loan Act Repeal and Payday Lending Ban
statute_links.description: Ga. Code Ann. § 16-8-1 et seq. - Criminalizes payday-style lending as felony racketeering. Effective repeal of industrial loan act authorization.

statute_links.name: Georgia Credit Services Organization Act
statute_links.description: Ga. Code Ann. § 34-10-1 et seq. - Regulates credit repair companies, prohibits upfront fees, requires licensing and consumer protections.

statute_links.name: Georgia Fair Lending Act
statute_links.description: Ga. Code Ann. § 34-60-1 et seq. - Regulates high-cost home loans and requires specific disclosures and protections for residential mortgages.

statute_links.name: Georgia Residential Mortgage Fraud Act
statute_links.description: Ga. Code Ann. § 16-8-10 - Addresses mortgage fraud and predatory lending practices.

statute_links.name: Georgia Usury Laws
statute_links.description: Ga. Code Ann. § 34-29-1 et seq. - Sets maximum interest rates (5% simple interest / 7% contract rate) for unlicensed lenders.

statute_links.name: Georgia Non-Judicial Foreclosure Law
statute_links.description: Ga. Code Ann. § 44-14-1 et seq. - Governs mortgage foreclosure procedures in Georgia (non-judicial state).

GA.recent_changes: No significant legislative changes affecting consumer lending or credit repair laws in Georgia in 2024-2025. The payday lending ban and criminalization under felony racketeering statutes remain in effect. Ongoing enforcement focus by the Georgia Attorney General on predatory lending and credit repair fraud violations.

HI.name: Hawaii
HI.usury_cap: 12% for consumer loans; payday loans capped at $600 with 15% fee
HI.consumer_protection_agency: Hawaii Attorney General Office of Consumer Protection
key_regulations: Payday loans capped at $600 with a maximum fee of 15% of the loan amount.
Borrowers limited to one payday loan at a time from any single lender.
The Hawaii Uniform Consumer Credit Code regulates consumer lending broadly.
Payday loan rollovers are prohibited.
HI.consumer_rights_summary: Hawaii permits payday lending with a $600 cap and 15% fee limit. Rollovers are prohibited and borrowers are limited to one loan per lender at a time. Consumers can file complaints with the Division of Financial Institutions or the Office of Consumer Protection.

credit_repair_laws.state_statute: Hawaii Credit Repair Services Organization Act
key_provisions: Credit repair companies must provide consumers with a written contract before any services are performed, clearly disclosing all terms and conditions.
Companies are prohibited from charging or collecting fees until and unless the promised services have been fully performed.
Credit repair companies must disclose that consumers have the right to sue for violations and may obtain damages, court costs, and attorney's fees.
Misrepresentation of services, results, or the consumer's rights is prohibited; companies cannot guarantee results.
Consumers have a three-day right to cancel any contract without penalty or obligation.
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: Hawaii Attorney General Office of Consumer Protection

personal_loans.rate_cap: 12% APR for consumer loans under Hawaii Uniform Consumer Credit Code
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans are regulated under the Hawaii Uniform Consumer Credit Code (HI Rev. Stat. § 476-1 et seq.), which sets a 12% usury cap for consumer loans. Lenders must be licensed with the Division of Financial Institutions.

payday_loans.rate_cap: 15% fee cap on loan amount
payday_loans.notes: Payday loans are legal in Hawaii with a maximum loan amount of $600 and a maximum fee of 15% of the loan amount. Borrowers are limited to one payday loan per lender at any given time. Payday loan rollovers are strictly prohibited. Lenders must be licensed with the Hawaii Division of Financial Institutions.

title_loans.notes: Title loans (vehicle title loans) are not explicitly permitted under Hawaii law and are generally not offered in the state.

installment_loans.rate_cap: 12% APR under Hawaii Uniform Consumer Credit Code
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans are regulated under HI Rev. Stat. § 476-1 et seq. (Hawaii Uniform Consumer Credit Code). The 12% usury cap applies. Lenders must be licensed with the Hawaii Division of Financial Institutions and comply with truth-in-lending disclosures.

mortgage.notes: Hawaii mortgages are regulated under HI Rev. Stat. § 667-1 et seq. and Chapter 480 (Mortgage Loan Originator License Act). Lenders must be licensed; Hawaii permits both judicial and non-judicial foreclosures. Non-judicial foreclosure requires notice and a power-of-sale clause in the mortgage. Judicial foreclosure is also available. Hawaii has a one-year redemption period following judicial sale.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 3953) applies to all covered borrowers (active duty service members and dependents) nationwide, including Hawaii. The MLA caps the Military Annual Percentage Rate (MAPR) at 36%, covering interest, fees, and certain ancillary charges. Hawaii lenders are subject to MLA enforcement by the CFPB and the Military Lending Act Examination Unit.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3953) provides federal protections including: 6% interest cap on pre-service debts, foreclosure and eviction protections requiring court orders, lease termination rights without penalty, and other financial protections for active duty service members. Hawaii courts must apply SCRA protections.
state_specific_protections: Hawaii Property Tax Exemption: Disabled veterans, surviving spouses, and surviving children may be exempt from property taxes on their primary residences under HI Rev. Stat. § 246-16.
Hawaii Veterans Affairs Division provides information, benefits counseling, and referrals to state and federal veterans' programs.
Hawaii has a dedicated Veterans' Services Officer in the Department of Defense who assists with claims processing and benefits navigation.
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: Hawaii Housing Finance and Development Corporation (HHFDC) offers VA loan programs and low-interest mortgages for eligible veterans. VA loans are not subject to state usury caps. Property tax exemptions available for 100% disabled veterans and their surviving spouses.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Major military installations in Hawaii: Joint Base Pearl Harbor-Hickam (Army, Navy, Air Force), Naval Base Kitsap (Submarine Base), Naval Submarine Base Guam, Schofield Barracks, Wheeler Army Airfield, Bellows Air Station, and Tripler Army Medical Center. These installations house significant active duty populations.

complaint_resources.name: Hawaii Division of Financial Institutions
complaint_resources.description: Regulates and licenses payday lenders, consumer loan companies, mortgage loan originators, and other financial service providers. Handles complaints regarding unlicensed lending, usury violations, and payday loan regulatory breaches.

complaint_resources.name: Hawaii Attorney General Office of Consumer Protection
complaint_resources.description: Enforces consumer protection laws, including credit repair company violations, unfair lending practices, and deceptive trade practices. Accepts complaints and investigates violations of the Hawaii Consumer Protection Act.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Handles federal consumer finance complaints including payday loans, mortgages, credit reporting, debt collection, and Military Lending Act violations. Accepts online and mail complaints.

complaint_resources.name: Hawaii Department of Defense, Veterans Services Office
complaint_resources.description: Provides assistance to veterans regarding benefits, housing programs, and can help address predatory lending targeting military personnel.

statute_links.name: Hawaii Uniform Consumer Credit Code
statute_links.description: Comprehensive regulation of consumer lending, establishing the 12% usury cap for consumer loans and governing terms of credit transactions.

statute_links.name: Hawaii Payday Loan Regulations (HI Rev. Stat. § 480-2.3)
statute_links.description: Specifically regulates payday loans, including the $600 cap, 15% fee limit, one-loan-per-lender restriction, and prohibition on rollovers.

statute_links.name: Hawaii Credit Services Organization Act
statute_links.description: Regulates credit repair and credit services companies, requiring written contracts, prohibiting upfront fees, and providing consumer cancellation rights.

statute_links.name: Hawaii Mortgage Loan Originator License Act
statute_links.description: Establishes licensing requirements and regulatory standards for mortgage loan originators and lenders in Hawaii.

statute_links.name: Hawaii Consumer Protection Act
statute_links.description: Broad consumer protection statute prohibiting unfair and deceptive practices in consumer transactions, including lending and credit services.

statute_links.name: Hawaii Property Tax Exemption for Veterans
statute_links.description: Provides property tax exemption for disabled veterans, surviving spouses, and qualifying dependents on primary residences.

HI.recent_changes: No significant changes to Hawaii consumer lending or credit repair laws in 2024-2025. The payday loan regulatory framework established in prior years remains in effect with the $600 cap and 15% fee limit. Regulatory focus continues on enforcement of existing usury caps and payday loan restrictions through the Division of Financial Institutions.

ID.name: Idaho
ID.usury_cap: No usury cap for written agreements; payday loans legal with no rate cap
ID.consumer_protection_agency: Idaho Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
key_regulations: Idaho has no usury cap when interest rates are set in a written agreement.
Payday lenders must be licensed but face no APR cap, leading to rates exceeding 400%.
The Idaho Credit Code governs consumer credit transactions.
Payday loans limited to $1,000 or 25% of gross monthly income, whichever is less.
ID.consumer_rights_summary: Idaho allows payday lending with minimal restrictions and no interest rate cap on written agreements. Loans are limited to $1,000 or 25% of gross monthly income. Consumers should exercise caution and can file complaints with the Idaho Department of Finance or the Attorney General.

credit_repair_laws.state_statute: Idaho Collection Agency Act — Credit Repair Organization Provisions
key_provisions: Credit services organizations must provide clients with a written contract before requesting payment or providing services
Organizations are prohibited from charging upfront fees before delivering promised credit repair services
Must disclose all material terms, conditions, and representations in plain language
Cannot make false or misleading claims about ability to improve credit reports or scores
Client has unconditional right to cancel contract within 5 business days without penalty
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: Idaho Department of Finance

personal_loans.rate_cap: No specific usury cap for written agreements under Idaho law; however, loans under Idaho Credit Code are subject to general consumer protection standards
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans are largely unregulated in Idaho when parties agree in writing. The Idaho Credit Code (Idaho Code § 28-45-101 et seq.) governs consumer credit transactions but does not establish an interest rate ceiling for written agreements.

payday_loans.rate_cap: No APR cap; lenders may charge any rate agreed to in writing
payday_loans.notes: Payday loans limited to $1,000 or 25% of gross monthly income (whichever is less). Lenders must be licensed by Idaho Department of Finance. No APR cap or rate restrictions apply, allowing rates exceeding 400%. No rollover restrictions or cooling-off periods mandated by state law.

title_loans.rate_cap: No specific cap; subject to general written agreement principle
title_loans.notes: Title loans are permitted in Idaho with minimal regulation. Interest rates are not capped when agreed to in writing, consistent with Idaho's general usury law exemption for written agreements.

installment_loans.rate_cap: No specific cap for written agreements; governed by Idaho Credit Code § 28-45-101 et seq.
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans are legal and largely unregulated regarding interest rates when properly documented. Consumer protection provisions apply regarding disclosure, contract terms, and prohibited practices.

mortgage.notes: Idaho mortgages are subject to Idaho Code § 45-1401 et seq. (Mortgages and Deeds of Trust). Idaho uses non-judicial foreclosure for deeds of trust (majority instrument) with notice requirements of at least 120 days. Mortgage lenders must be licensed. Fair lending laws and disclosure requirements under federal law (TRID, ECOA) apply.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The federal Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 987) caps the MAPR (Military Annual Percentage Rate) at 36% for covered borrowers (active duty service members and their dependents). This applies to payday loans, title loans, and installment loans in Idaho. Lenders must obtain a signed certification of status and cannot require a waiver of MLA protections.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3953) provides covered service members with a 6% interest rate cap on pre-service debts, foreclosure/eviction protections including stay rights and notice requirements, and lease termination rights. SCRA protections apply in Idaho regardless of state law and are enforced by federal courts and the Department of Justice.
state_specific_protections: Idaho does not have state-specific additional interest rate caps for military borrowers beyond federal MLA protection

Idaho recognizes federal tax exemptions and benefits for service members but does not provide state-level property tax exemptions specifically for veterans on primary residences (though some local jurisdictions may offer exemptions)
Disabled veterans may be eligible for property tax exemptions under Idaho Code § 63-602 based on disability rating from VA
Idaho maintains a Veterans Services Bureau within the Department of Veterans Affairs providing education, benefits counseling, and referrals to financial assistance programs
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: Idaho recognizes VA-backed loans (VA mortgages) with no state-imposed additional restrictions beyond federal VA loan requirements. The Idaho Housing and Finance Association (IHFA) offers state bond programs but does not specifically restrict VA loan availability. Property tax exemptions for disabled veterans are available through Idaho Code § 63-602 based on VA disability rating (typically 10% or more).
veteran_protections.military_installations: Mountain Home Air Force Base (Mountain Home, ID) — primary military installation in the state; also Farragut Naval Training Facility historical site

complaint_resources.name: Idaho Department of Finance
complaint_resources.description: Licenses and regulates payday lenders, credit services organizations, and other financial service providers. Handles complaints regarding unlicensed lending, usurious practices, and violations of Idaho lending laws.

complaint_resources.name: Idaho Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
complaint_resources.description: Investigates consumer complaints, enforces consumer protection statutes, and takes action against deceptive lending practices, credit repair fraud, and unfair business practices.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints about payday loans, title loans, credit reporting, debt collection, and other consumer financial products. Complaint data is used to identify patterns of misconduct.

complaint_resources.name: Idaho Department of Veterans Affairs
complaint_resources.description: Provides resources and referrals for veterans experiencing financial hardship or predatory lending; offers benefits counseling and assistance navigating financial services.

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
complaint_resources.description: Collects complaints about credit repair scams, identity theft, and fraudulent financial practices. Works with state and federal partners to enforce consumer protection laws.

statute_links.name: Idaho Credit Code (Unsecured Personal Loans and Related Transactions)
statute_links.description: Comprehensive regulation of consumer credit transactions, including disclosure requirements, contract terms, prohibited practices, and remedies. Does not establish interest rate cap for written agreements.

statute_links.name: Idaho Credit Services Organization Act
statute_links.description: Regulates credit repair and credit services organizations; requires bonding, registration, written contracts, prohibition on upfront fees, and 5-day cancellation rights.

statute_links.name: Idaho Payday Loan Regulations (Idaho Code § 28-45-801 to 28-45-835)
statute_links.description: Establishes licensing requirements for payday lenders, maximum loan amounts ($1,000 or 25% of monthly income), and consumer protections. Contains no rate cap, allowing lenders to charge unlimited interest on written agreements.

statute_links.name: Idaho Mortgage and Deed of Trust Law
statute_links.description: Governs residential and commercial mortgages, foreclosure procedures (non-judicial), notice requirements, and lender licensing. Requires minimum 120-day notice before non-judicial foreclosure sale.

statute_links.name: Idaho Property Tax Exemptions for Disabled Veterans
statute_links.description: Provides property tax exemptions for disabled veterans with VA disability rating; exemption amounts vary by disability percentage under Idaho Code § 63-602.

statute_links.name: Military Lending Act (Federal)
statute_links.description: Federal law capping MAPR at 36% for covered active duty service members and their dependents. Applies in Idaho and preempts state law when more restrictive.

statute_links.name: Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (Federal)
statute_links.description: Federal law providing 6% interest rate cap on pre-service debts, foreclosure protections, and lease termination rights for covered service members. Applies nationwide including Idaho.

ID.recent_changes: No significant changes to Idaho consumer lending or credit repair law in 2024-2025. Idaho's regulatory framework remains largely unchanged since the establishment of the Credit Services Organization Act and payday loan licensing structure in prior years. Consumers should monitor updates from the Idaho Department of Finance regarding enforcement actions or regulatory guidance.

IL.name: Illinois
IL.usury_cap: 36% APR cap on all consumer loans (Illinois Predatory Loan Prevention Act, 2021)
IL.consumer_protection_agency: Illinois Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
IL.licensing_board: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation
key_regulations: The Predatory Loan Prevention Act (2021) caps all consumer loans at 36% APR including fees.
Traditional payday loans are effectively eliminated due to the 36% cap.
The Consumer Installment Loan Act regulates installment lending with additional protections.
Military members receive additional protections under the Military Lending Act.
IL.consumer_rights_summary: Illinois enacted the Predatory Loan Prevention Act in 2021, capping all consumer loans at 36% APR including fees, effectively banning traditional payday lending. The DFPR enforces comprehensive lending regulations. Consumers can file complaints online with DFPR or the Attorney General's office.

key_provisions: Credit repair organizations must provide written contract clearly stating all terms, conditions, and cancellation rights before consumer pays any fee
Prohibits charging or collecting any fee before services are fully performed and results are achieved
Requires written disclosure of consumer's right to dispute items directly with credit reporting agencies at no cost
Mandates inclusion of the consumer's three-day cancellation right in all contracts
Prohibits misrepresenting services or making false claims about ability to remove accurate negative information
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR)

personal_loans.rate_cap: 36% APR cap (including all fees) under Illinois Predatory Loan Prevention Act (2021)
personal_loans.notes: All consumer loans are capped at 36% APR including fees and charges. Applies to all lenders offering personal loans to Illinois residents.

payday_loans.rate_cap: Effectively eliminated by 36% APR cap under Predatory Loan Prevention Act
payday_loans.notes: Traditional payday loans are not permitted in Illinois. The 36% APR cap on all consumer loans, including fees, makes the payday loan business model legally unviable. Lenders previously offering payday loans must comply with the APR cap or exit the market.

title_loans.rate_cap: Subject to 36% APR cap; effectively restricted
title_loans.notes: Title loans are not explicitly prohibited but are heavily restricted and effectively eliminated by the 36% APR cap on all consumer loans including fees. Vehicle title loans are rarely offered in Illinois due to this regulatory constraint.

installment_loans.rate_cap: 36% APR cap (including all fees) under Illinois Predatory Loan Prevention Act (2021)
installment_loans.notes: Regulated under the Illinois Consumer Installment Loan Act (815 ILCS 601/1 et seq.). Installment loans must comply with the 36% APR cap. Lenders must disclose all terms clearly and provide notice of cancellation rights where applicable.

mortgage.notes: Mortgages are regulated by the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation. Illinois requires judicial foreclosure (filed in court). Mortgagors have redemption rights post-foreclosure in certain circumstances. Federal RESPA and TILA apply. State licensing required for mortgage loan originators under the Residential Mortgage License Act (815 ILCS 137/1 et seq.).

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The federal Military Lending Act (MLA) caps all consumer credit to active duty service members at 36% MAPR (Military Annual Percentage Rate), including all fees and charges. This federal cap applies in Illinois and is enforced by the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. Covered borrowers receive enhanced disclosures and cannot be charged finance charges on late fees. MLA provides a safe harbor for lenders complying with its requirements.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (SCRA) provides active duty service members with a 6% interest rate cap on pre-service debts (credit cards, personal loans, mortgages incurred before entering service). SCRA also provides foreclosure protections, lease termination rights, protection from default judgments without appearance, and reduced rent on residential leases. Protections apply in Illinois courts and through servicer compliance nationwide.
state_specific_protections: Illinois incorporates federal MLA and SCRA protections into state lending law; the state's 36% APR cap aligns with and complements the federal 36% MAPR cap for active duty service members
The Illinois Attorney General's office enforces compliance with federal and state protections for service members
Illinois law recognizes military service as a factor in certain consumer protection disputes and provides additional remedies for violations of federal service member protections
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: Illinois participates in the VA loan program with no state-specific restrictions or additional requirements beyond federal guidelines. Illinois offers a Property Tax Exemption for disabled veterans (35% to 100% exemption based on disability rating) administered by individual county assessors. The Illinois Department of Veterans Affairs provides resources for VA benefits, housing assistance, and financial counseling for veterans.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Major military installations in Illinois include: Naval Station Great Lakes (Great Lakes, IL) - largest naval training center; Scott Air Force Base (Belleville, IL); Fort Leonard Wood has training pipelines from Illinois; multiple Illinois Army National Guard and Air National Guard facilities throughout the state.

complaint_resources.name: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (DFPR)
complaint_resources.description: Enforces the Predatory Loan Prevention Act, Consumer Installment Loan Act, Credit Services Organization Act, and licenses lenders. Handles complaints about lending violations, APR cap violations, and credit repair company misconduct.

complaint_resources.name: Illinois Attorney General - Consumer Protection Division
complaint_resources.description: Enforces consumer protection laws and can file complaints online. Handles predatory lending complaints, credit repair fraud, and unfair/deceptive lending practices. Operates consumer hotline: 1-877-5-CRIMES.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Federal agency that accepts complaints about consumer financial products and services including loans, credit repair services, and Military Lending Act violations. Provides multi-language support and tracks national trends.

complaint_resources.name: Illinois Department of Veterans Affairs
complaint_resources.description: Provides resources, benefits counseling, and financial assistance programs for veterans. Can assist with complaints involving military lending violations or veteran-specific financial issues.

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints about credit repair fraud, identity theft, and unfair lending practices. Investigates and pursues enforcement actions against violators.

statute_links.name: Illinois Predatory Loan Prevention Act (2021)
statute_links.description: Caps all consumer loans at 36% APR including all fees and charges; effectively eliminates payday lending in Illinois. Signed into law in 2021, effective for loans originated after July 1, 2022.

statute_links.name: Illinois Consumer Installment Loan Act (815 ILCS 601/1 et seq.)
statute_links.description: Regulates installment lending with disclosure requirements, rate caps, and consumer protections. Works in conjunction with the Predatory Loan Prevention Act.

statute_links.name: Illinois Credit Services Organization Act (815 ILCS 605/1 et seq.)
statute_links.description: Governs credit repair companies, requiring registration, contracts, written disclosures, and prohibiting upfront fees. Provides three-day cancellation rights.

statute_links.name: Illinois Residential Mortgage License Act (815 ILCS 137/1 et seq.)
statute_links.description: Requires licensing for mortgage loan originators and establishes standards for mortgage lending practices and disclosures.

statute_links.name: Military Lending Act (10 U.S.C. § 987) - Federal
statute_links.description: Federal law capping MAPR at 36% for active duty service members, enforced nationwide including in Illinois by the CFPB.

statute_links.name: Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3901 et seq.) - Federal
statute_links.description: Federal law providing 6% interest cap on pre-service debts and foreclosure protections for active duty service members, enforced nationwide.

IL.recent_changes: The Illinois Predatory Loan Prevention Act (2021) represents the most significant recent change, capping all consumer loans at 36% APR including fees and effectively eliminating payday lending in the state as of July 1, 2022. No additional major lending or credit repair legislation changes reported in 2024-2025.

IN.name: Indiana
IN.usury_cap: 36% for first $2,000 (small loans); payday loans capped at $605 with tiered fees
IN.consumer_protection_agency: Indiana Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
key_regulations: Payday loans capped at $605 with tiered fee structure: 15% on first $250, 13% on $251-$400, 10% on $401-$605.
Borrowers may have up to two payday loans simultaneously but not from the same lender.
A statewide database tracks all payday loans.
The Uniform Consumer Credit Code provides broad consumer lending protections.
IN.consumer_rights_summary: Indiana allows payday lending with a $605 cap and tiered fee structure. A statewide database prevents excessive borrowing. The Department of Financial Institutions regulates all consumer lenders, and complaints can be filed with the DFI or the Attorney General's Consumer Protection Division.

key_provisions: Credit repair companies must provide a written contract clearly stating services, fees, timeline, and cancellation rights before payment
Prohibits collection of fees until services are actually performed and results are delivered to the consumer
Requires companies to inform consumers of their right to obtain free credit reports and dispute inaccuracies directly with credit bureaus
Prohibits making false or misleading claims about the company's ability to improve credit rating or remove accurate negative information
Mandates a 3-day cancellation period during which consumers may rescind the contract without penalty
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: Indiana Attorney General

personal_loans.rate_cap: 36% APR for loans up to $2,000 under small loan provisions; rates above $2,000 subject to Uniform Consumer Credit Code
personal_loans.notes: Regulated under the Uniform Consumer Credit Code (Ind. Code § 24-4.5). Personal loans are permitted with rate caps varying by loan amount and type.

payday_loans.rate_cap: Tiered fee structure: 15% on first $250; 13% on $251-$400; 10% on $401-$605
payday_loans.notes: Payday loans capped at $605 maximum. Borrowers may hold up to two payday loans simultaneously but not from the same lender. A statewide database (operated by DFI) tracks all outstanding payday loans to prevent over-borrowing. Regulated under Ind. Code § 24-4.5-1-101 et seq.

title_loans.notes: Title loans are not explicitly authorized under Indiana law and are effectively prohibited through consumer lending regulations.

installment_loans.rate_cap: Governed by Uniform Consumer Credit Code with rate caps of 36% APR for loans under $2,000; higher amounts may have different rate structures
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans are regulated under Ind. Code § 24-4.5. Creditors must disclose all terms, finance charges, and payment schedules in writing. The DFI oversees compliance.

mortgage.notes: Indiana mortgages are regulated under state and federal law. Foreclosures are judicial in nature, requiring court proceedings. Indiana requires licensing for mortgage loan originators under Ind. Code § 24-4.5-2. The state implements RESPA, TILA, and HOEPA requirements. Homeowners have statutory right to cure default. The DFI licenses and regulates mortgage lenders and servicers.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (50 U.S.C. § 3953) caps the Military Annual Percentage Rate (MAPR) at 36% for covered borrowers on payday loans, title loans, and installment loans. Indiana payday loans (capped at tiered fees equivalent to roughly 15-36% depending on amount) may fall within or exceed MAPR limits depending on the loan amount; servicemembers should verify compliance. The MAPR includes all fees and interest.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3953) provides active duty servicemembers with a 6% interest rate cap on pre-service debts, protection from foreclosure and eviction (120-day stay period), lease termination rights, and reduced liability on contracts. These protections apply nationwide in Indiana and supersede higher state rates for covered service members.
state_specific_protections: Indiana recognizes SCRA and MLA protections; servicemembers may file complaints with the DFI regarding lending violations
The Indiana Attorney General's Consumer Protection Division investigates predatory lending practices targeting military families
Indiana does not impose additional state-level military lending restrictions beyond federal requirements; federal protections are primary
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: Indiana participates in VA home loan programs. Veterans may be eligible for property tax deductions under the Veteran's Standard Deduction (Ind. Code § 6-1.1-12-36.7) for primary residences. The Indiana Housing and Community Development Authority (IHCDA) manages state housing programs including down payment assistance and refinancing programs for veterans. VA loans in Indiana follow federal guidelines with no additional state loan origination licensing beyond standard mortgage requirements.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Major installations: Naval Surface Warfare Center at Crane (Lawrence County), Fort Benjamin Harrison area (Indianapolis/Marion County), Indiana National Guard facilities statewide. These areas are focus areas for Military Lending Act enforcement.

complaint_resources.name: Indiana Department of Financial Institutions (DFI)
complaint_resources.description: Regulates all consumer lenders, payday loan providers, credit repair companies, mortgage lenders, and loan servicers. Accepts complaints regarding unlicensed lending, excessive fees, predatory practices, database tracking violations, and regulatory non-compliance.

complaint_resources.name: Indiana Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
complaint_resources.description: Handles consumer protection complaints including deceptive lending practices, fraud, and violations of consumer credit laws. Investigates complaints and pursues enforcement actions against violators.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Federal agency handling complaints about payday loans, personal loans, mortgages, credit repair services, and other consumer financial products. Maintains complaint database and coordinates with state regulators.

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
complaint_resources.description: Handles complaints about credit repair fraud, identity theft, deceptive lending practices, and other consumer financial fraud matters.

complaint_resources.name: Indiana Better Business Bureau
complaint_resources.description: Accreditation and complaint resolution for consumer lending and credit service providers operating in Indiana.

statute_links.name: Uniform Consumer Credit Code (UCCCC)
statute_links.description: Indiana Code § 24-4.5 governs personal loans, payday loans, installment loans, rate caps, and consumer protections. Core lending regulations.

statute_links.name: Indiana Credit Services Organizations Act
statute_links.description: Indiana Code § 24-5-15 regulates credit repair companies, requires contracts, prohibits upfront fees, mandates 3-day cancellation period, and registration with the Attorney General.

statute_links.name: Indiana Payday Loan Regulations
statute_links.description: Detailed payday loan provisions including $605 cap, tiered fee structure, database tracking requirements, and limitations on simultaneous loans.

statute_links.name: Indiana Mortgage Licensing Law
statute_links.description: Indiana Code § 24-4.5-2 requires licensing for mortgage loan originators and lenders; establishes lending standards and consumer disclosure requirements.

statute_links.name: Indiana Consumer Protection Act
statute_links.description: Indiana Code § 24-5-0.5 provides general consumer protection framework and allows the Attorney General to prosecute unfair/deceptive practices in lending.

statute_links.name: Military Lending Act (Federal)
statute_links.description: 50 U.S.C. § 3953 caps MAPR at 36% for covered military borrowers; applies nationwide including Indiana.

statute_links.name: Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (Federal)
statute_links.description: 50 U.S.C. § 3953 et seq. provides 6% interest rate cap, foreclosure/eviction protections, and other benefits for active duty servicemembers; supersedes state law in Indiana.

IN.recent_changes: No significant legislative changes to Indiana consumer lending or credit repair laws in 2024-2025. The state continues to enforce existing regulations under the Uniform Consumer Credit Code and payday loan provisions established in prior years. Regulatory focus remains on database tracking compliance and prevention of illegal lending.

IA.name: Iowa
IA.usury_cap: 21% for consumer loans over $30,000; tiered rates for smaller loans; payday loans regulated
IA.consumer_protection_agency: Iowa Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
key_regulations: Payday loans capped at $500 with maximum fee of $15 per $100 for first $300 and $10 per $100 above.
Maximum payday loan term is 31 days.
Licensed delayed deposit services may not allow rollovers.
The Iowa Consumer Credit Code governs all consumer lending with tiered rate caps.
IA.consumer_rights_summary: Iowa permits payday lending with a $500 cap, fee limits, and no rollovers. The Iowa Consumer Credit Code provides comprehensive protections for all consumer credit transactions. Consumers can file complaints with the Iowa Division of Banking or the Attorney General.

key_provisions: Credit services organizations must provide written contract with detailed explanation of services, costs, and consumer rights before charging any fee
Prohibited from charging upfront fees before services are actually delivered and results are achieved
Must clearly disclose that consumer has the right to dispute items with credit reporting agencies without using a credit repair service
Cannot misrepresent services or make false claims about ability to improve credit scores or remove accurate negative information
Must provide consumer with copies of all documents submitted to credit reporting agencies and maintain records of services performed
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: Iowa Secretary of State

personal_loans.rate_cap: 21% APR for consumer loans over $30,000; tiered rates for loans under $30,000 (rates increase as loan amount decreases, with higher caps for smaller loans)
personal_loans.notes: Governed by Iowa Consumer Credit Code (Iowa Code § 537.1 et seq.). Rates for loans under $30,000 vary by amount; lenders must comply with specific tiered rate schedules established in state law.

payday_loans.rate_cap: Maximum fee of $15 per $100 borrowed for the first $300; maximum fee of $10 per $100 borrowed for amounts above $300
payday_loans.notes: Licensed under Iowa Code § 536C. Rollovers explicitly prohibited. Maximum loan term is 31 days. Lenders must be licensed by Iowa Division of Banking. No state-level database tracking requirement, but federal OFAC and other identity verification required.

title_loans.notes: Title loans are not explicitly authorized under Iowa law. Vehicles securing loans must comply with uniform commercial code provisions, but auto title lending as a distinct product is not regulated as a separate category and is effectively not permitted as a dedicated lending product.

installment_loans.rate_cap: Tiered rates under Iowa Consumer Credit Code; 21% APR maximum for loans over $30,000; lower maximums for smaller loan amounts
installment_loans.notes: Governed by Iowa Code § 537.1 et seq. (Iowa Consumer Credit Code). Installment loans for personal, family, or household purposes are subject to state rate caps and disclosure requirements. Lenders must be licensed and comply with consumer protection provisions.

mortgage.notes: Iowa mortgages are governed by Iowa Code § 654.1 et seq. Foreclosures in Iowa are judicial (require court proceedings), providing additional consumer protections. Residential mortgage lenders and loan originators must be licensed through the Iowa Division of Banking. Home equity lending is regulated under Iowa Code § 537.1 et seq. Truth in Lending Act (TILA) and Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) apply at federal level.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 987) applies to service members and their dependents in Iowa, capping the Annual Percentage Rate (MAPR) at 36% for consumer credit products and prohibiting mandatory arbitration clauses. This applies to payday loans, title loans (where permitted), and other covered credit products used by active duty service members.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3953) provides active duty service members protection through a 6% interest rate cap on pre-service debts, foreclosure and eviction protections requiring court proceedings, and lease termination rights. These protections apply in Iowa regardless of state law.
state_specific_protections: Iowa provides property tax exemptions for veterans with service-connected disabilities (Iowa Code § 426.3), reducing property tax burden for eligible veterans
Iowa Division of Banking enforces Military Lending Act compliance and has authority to investigate violations; veterans and service members can file complaints with the division
Iowa offers Veterans Trust Fund and other state benefits programs, though credit-specific protections are primarily federal through MLA and SCRA
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: Iowa does not have a state-specific VA loan program; however, VA loans (backed by the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs) are available to eligible veterans in Iowa and are not subject to state usury caps. Iowa honors VA loan modifications and forbearance agreements. The state Housing Finance Authority may partner with VA programs for secondary market support.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Major military installations in Iowa include Camp Dodge (National Guard facility near Johnston), and proximity to Offutt Air Force Base in neighboring Nebraska. MLA enforcement typically focuses on lenders near military concentrations.

complaint_resources.name: Iowa Division of Banking
complaint_resources.description: Handles licensing and complaints against payday lenders, credit repair services, mortgage lenders, and other financial service providers. Enforces Iowa Consumer Credit Code and payday loan regulations.

complaint_resources.name: Iowa Attorney General - Consumer Protection Division
complaint_resources.description: Investigates consumer fraud, deceptive practices, and unfair lending practices. Handles complaints about credit repair fraud, predatory lending, and violations of consumer protection laws.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints about payday loans, credit repair services, mortgages, and other consumer financial products. Investigates federal TILA, FCRA, FDCPA, and other consumer protection law violations.

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
complaint_resources.description: Handles complaints about credit repair scams, identity theft, and deceptive lending practices. Works with state attorneys general on enforcement actions.

statute_links.name: Iowa Consumer Credit Code
statute_links.description: Comprehensive state law governing personal loans, installment loans, and consumer credit transactions, including rate caps and disclosure requirements (Iowa Code § 537.1 et seq.)

statute_links.name: Iowa Payday Loan Regulation
statute_links.description: Specific regulation of payday loans, including licensing requirements, fee caps, and loan limits (Iowa Code § 536C)

statute_links.name: Iowa Credit Services Organization Act
statute_links.description: Regulation of credit repair companies, including bonding, registration, upfront fee prohibitions, and consumer rights (Iowa Code § 538A.1 - 538A.12)

statute_links.name: Iowa Mortgage Lending Regulation
statute_links.description: Foreclosure procedures and mortgage lending rules in Iowa, establishing judicial foreclosure requirement (Iowa Code § 654.1 et seq.)

statute_links.name: Iowa Division of Banking Rules
statute_links.description: Administrative rules implementing state lending and banking laws, including licensing application procedures and compliance standards

IA.recent_changes: No significant changes to Iowa's core payday loan or consumer credit lending laws in 2024-2025. The state continues to enforce existing tiered rate caps under the Iowa Consumer Credit Code and the $500 payday loan cap with fee limitations first established in 2008. Recent regulatory focus has been on compliance enforcement by the Iowa Division of Banking rather than new legislation.

KS.name: Kansas
KS.usury_cap: 15% for agreements; payday loans capped at $500 with $15 per $100 fee
KS.consumer_protection_agency: Kansas Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
KS.licensing_board: Kansas Office of the State Bank Commissioner
key_regulations: Payday loans capped at $500 with a maximum fee of $15 per $100.
Cash advance loans limited to 30-day terms with no rollovers.
Licensed supervised lenders can charge rates exceeding the usury cap.
The Kansas Consumer Protection Act prohibits deceptive lending practices.
KS.consumer_rights_summary: Kansas allows payday lending with a $500 cap and $15 per $100 fee limit. Rollovers are prohibited. The Office of the State Bank Commissioner regulates consumer lenders, and complaints can be filed with the OSBC or the Attorney General's Consumer Protection Division.

key_provisions: Credit repair organizations must provide consumers with a written contract before performing services, clearly stating the nature of services, total cost, and payment terms.
Upfront fees are prohibited; payment must be collected only after services are actually performed.
Organizations must disclose that consumers have the right to dispute inaccurate information directly with credit reporting agencies at no cost.
A written notice of cancellation rights must be provided; consumers can cancel within 3 business days without penalty.
Credit repair companies cannot charge fees for disputing information or for obtaining consumer credit reports.
False or misleading advertising regarding credit repair services is prohibited under Kansas Consumer Protection Act.
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: Kansas Office of the State Bank Commissioner

personal_loans.rate_cap: 15% APR for most consumer lending agreements; licensed supervised lenders may exceed usury cap
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans regulated under Kansas consumer lending laws. Licensed lenders have greater flexibility on rates. Unlicensed lenders bound by 15% usury cap.

payday_loans.rate_cap: $15 per $100 borrowed (approximately 156% APR on typical 2-week loans)
payday_loans.notes: Payday loans capped at $500 maximum. 30-day term limit with no rollovers permitted. Licensed under Office of the State Bank Commissioner. Fees strictly limited to $15 per $100 of loan amount.

title_loans.notes: Title loans are not explicitly authorized under Kansas law and are effectively prohibited.

installment_loans.rate_cap: 15% APR usury cap applies; licensed lenders may charge higher rates
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans for consumer purposes are legal under Kansas law. Governed by the Kansas Consumer Protection Act and regulated by the Office of the State Bank Commissioner.

mortgage.notes: Kansas mortgages regulated under state law with foreclosure by judicial process (court action required). Licensed mortgage lenders subject to regulation by state banking authority. Homestead exemption available; specific amount depends on property classification. High-cost home loans subject to additional protections under Kansas Residential Mortgage Act.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The federal Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 987) applies to all active duty service members and their dependents in Kansas. The law caps the Military Annual Percentage Rate (MAPR) at 36% for covered borrowers and prohibits certain abusive practices including mandatory arbitration, wage garnishment, and vehicle title holding.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act applies nationwide. Active duty service members in Kansas are protected by: 6% interest rate cap on debts incurred before active duty service, foreclosure and eviction protections, lease termination rights, and stay of court proceedings.
state_specific_protections: Kansas Property Tax Homestead Exemption: Disabled veterans and surviving spouses of disabled veterans may qualify for property tax exemptions on their primary residence.
Kansas Veterans Tuition Benefit: While not directly lending-related, the state offers educational benefits to veterans which may reduce need for education loans.
Kansas Military Family Relief Fund: Provides emergency assistance to military families in financial hardship.
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: Kansas has no state-specific VA loan program but recognizes federal VA loan benefits. Lenders are required to honor VA loan terms. Kansas allows veterans to use VA loans for primary residences throughout the state with no additional state restrictions.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Fort Riley (Army installation in Junction City, Riley County) and McConnell Air Force Base (Wichita, Sedgwick County) are major military installations where federal MLA protections are actively enforced.

complaint_resources.name: Kansas Office of the State Bank Commissioner
complaint_resources.description: Regulates consumer lenders, payday lenders, and other financial service providers in Kansas. Handles complaints regarding lending practices, licensing violations, and usury violations.

complaint_resources.name: Kansas Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
complaint_resources.description: Enforces the Kansas Consumer Protection Act including lending fraud, deceptive practices, credit repair violations, and unfair debt collection. Accepts consumer complaints.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Federal agency accepting complaints about consumer financial products and services including payday loans, personal loans, mortgage servicers, and credit reporting.

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints regarding credit repair scams, identity theft, and deceptive lending practices.

statute_links.name: Kansas Consumer Protection Act
statute_links.description: Kan. Stat. Ann. § 50-623 et seq. Comprehensive consumer protection law covering deceptive lending practices, unfair debt collection, credit services, and consumer rights.

statute_links.name: Kansas Payday Loan Law
statute_links.description: Kan. Stat. Ann. § 16a-2-405 et seq. Governs payday lending with $500 cap, $15 per $100 fee limit, 30-day term, and rollover prohibition.

statute_links.name: Kansas Usury Laws
statute_links.description: Kan. Stat. Ann. § 16-204. Sets baseline 15% APR usury cap for consumer lending agreements, with exceptions for licensed supervised lenders.

statute_links.name: Kansas Supervised Lender Act
statute_links.description: Kan. Stat. Ann. § 16a-1-101 et seq. Regulates consumer lending, installment loans, and provides licensing framework for lenders with rate flexibility.

statute_links.name: Kansas Residential Mortgage Act
statute_links.description: Kan. Stat. Ann. § 16-2301 et seq. Regulates high-cost mortgages and mortgage lenders; includes licensing and consumer protection requirements.

KS.recent_changes: No significant changes to Kansas consumer lending or credit repair laws were enacted in 2024-2025. The payday loan framework, usury cap, and consumer protection provisions remain substantially unchanged from prior years.

KY.name: Kentucky
KY.usury_cap: 19% for consumer loans over $15,000; payday loans capped at $500 with $15 per $100 fee
KY.consumer_protection_agency: Kentucky Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
key_regulations: Payday loans (deferred deposit transactions) capped at $500 with maximum $15 per $100 fee.
Borrowers limited to two outstanding payday loans at a time tracked via statewide database.
A mandatory 24-hour cooling-off period applies after the second consecutive loan.
The Kentucky Consumer Protection Act prohibits unfair and deceptive lending practices.
KY.consumer_rights_summary: Kentucky allows payday lending with a $500 cap, $15 per $100 fee limit, and a statewide tracking database. Borrowers are limited to two loans at a time with a cooling-off period. Consumers can file complaints with the Department of Financial Institutions or the Attorney General.

credit_repair_laws.state_statute: No standalone Credit Services Organization Act — federal CROA applies; state telephone solicitation law prohibits advance fees for credit repair (KRS 367.46955)
credit_repair_laws.statute_code: KRS § 367.46955 (advance fee prohibition via telephone solicitation)
key_provisions: Credit repair organizations must provide written contract before any services rendered, with clear disclosure of all terms, conditions, and consumer rights
Prohibition on charging or collecting fees before services are actually delivered and results are achieved
Credit repair companies must inform consumers of their right to dispute inaccurate credit information directly with credit bureaus at no cost
Contract must include a statement that the consumer can cancel within 5 days without penalty or obligation
Prohibition on making any representations about removing accurate negative information from credit reports
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: Kentucky Attorney General (enforcement only — no state CSO registration exists)

personal_loans.rate_cap: 19% APR for consumer loans over $15,000; rates on loans under $15,000 may be higher
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans are regulated under Kentucky's usury laws. Interest rates are capped at 19% APR for loans exceeding $15,000. Smaller personal loans may have different rate structures.

payday_loans.rate_cap: $15 per $100 borrowed
payday_loans.notes: Payday loans (deferred deposit transactions) capped at $500 with maximum $15 per $100 fee. Borrowers tracked via statewide database limited to two outstanding loans simultaneously. A mandatory 24-hour cooling-off period applies after the second consecutive loan. Licensed by Department of Financial Institutions.

title_loans.notes: Title loans are not permitted under Kentucky law. Vehicle title lending is prohibited to protect consumers from high-cost secured lending.

installment_loans.rate_cap: 19% APR for loans over $15,000; rates on smaller loans subject to individual creditor terms within usury limits
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans are regulated under Kentucky usury law and consumer protection statutes. Lenders must comply with disclosures under the Truth in Lending Act (TILA) and Regulation Z.

mortgage.notes: Kentucky mortgages are subject to judicial foreclosure requirements. Lenders must file suit in court to foreclose on residential property. Kentucky follows the Uniform Residential Mortgage Practices Act. Residential mortgage lenders must be licensed by the Kentucky Department of Financial Institutions. The state requires compliance with federal mortgage regulations including TILA, RESPA, Fair Lending Act, and ECOA.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 987) caps the Military Annual Percentage Rate (MAPR) at 36% for active duty service members, including all fees and interest. This applies to covered borrowers in Kentucky regardless of state law. The MLA also prohibits mandatory arbitration, requires clear disclosures, and limits garnishment to 25% of disposable pay.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3953) limits interest rates on pre-service debts to 6% during active duty military service. SCRA provides protections against foreclosure, eviction, and lease termination without court order during active service. Provides rent protections and limits vehicle repossession rights. Kentucky courts apply SCRA protections to eligible service members.
state_specific_protections: Kentucky provides property tax homestead exemptions for eligible veterans with service-connected disabilities rated by the VA
Kentucky Department of Veterans Services administers veteran benefits and assists with complaint resolution regarding lending discrimination against service members
Kentucky honors federal MLA and SCRA requirements with no state restrictions that would circumvent these protections
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: Kentucky participates in VA home loan guaranty program. The state has no restrictions on VA loans and recognizes VA loan benefits. Veterans may access property tax exemptions based on disability rating. Kentucky Housing Corporation offers programs supporting veteran homeownership.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Fort Knox (Major installation in Kentucky housing 30,000+ active duty and civilian personnel); Fort Campbell (shared with Tennessee); numerous Kentucky Air National Guard and Army National Guard facilities

complaint_resources.name: Kentucky Department of Financial Institutions
complaint_resources.description: Handles licensing and complaints against payday lenders, credit service organizations, mortgage lenders, and other regulated financial institutions in Kentucky

complaint_resources.name: Kentucky Attorney General - Consumer Protection Division
complaint_resources.description: Investigates complaints about unfair and deceptive lending practices, credit repair fraud, and violations of the Kentucky Consumer Protection Act

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints about credit cards, mortgages, student loans, payday loans, and other consumer financial products; refers state complaints to Kentucky regulators

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
complaint_resources.description: Collects complaints about identity theft, credit repair scams, and deceptive lending practices; shares data with Kentucky law enforcement

complaint_resources.name: Kentucky Legal Aid
complaint_resources.description: Provides free legal assistance to low-income Kentuckians with lending disputes, foreclosure defense, and consumer protection matters

statute_links.name: Kentucky Revised Statutes Chapter 365 (Debt Collection, Consumer Finance)
statute_links.description: Comprehensive Kentucky consumer finance law covering payday loans, credit services, consumer protection, and unfair practices

statute_links.name: Kentucky Telephone Solicitation — Credit Repair Advance Fee Prohibition (KRS § 367.46955)
statute_links.description: Kentucky does not have a standalone CSO act. KRS 367.46955 prohibits advance fees for credit repair via telephone solicitation. Federal CROA provides primary consumer protections.

statute_links.name: Kentucky Revised Statutes § 365.010-365.140 (Payday Loans/Deferred Deposit Transactions)
statute_links.description: Comprehensive payday loan regulation including $500 cap, $15 per $100 fee limit, statewide database tracking, and cooling-off period

statute_links.name: Kentucky Consumer Protection Act § 367.110 et seq.
statute_links.description: Prohibits unfair, unconscionable, and deceptive practices in consumer transactions including lending

statute_links.name: Kentucky Department of Financial Institutions - Administrative Regulations (806 KAR)
statute_links.description: Administrative rules and regulations for financial institutions and licensees in Kentucky

KY.recent_changes: Kentucky has maintained relatively stable consumer lending regulation in 2024-2025. The $500 payday loan cap and dual-loan limit with statewide database tracking have been consistent enforcement priorities. No major legislative changes to usury limits or payday loan structure occurred in recent session; regulatory focus remains on compliance with existing deferred deposit transaction rules through the Department of Financial Institutions.

LA.name: Louisiana
LA.usury_cap: 12% default rate; payday loans exempt with fees up to $20 per $100 ($350 max) or 16.75% per $100 ($350+)
LA.consumer_protection_agency: Louisiana Attorney General Consumer Protection Section
key_regulations: Payday loans capped at $350 with fee of $20 per $100; amounts over $350 (up to $500) at 16.75% per $100.
Loan term must be between 14-30 days.
Rollovers are limited; borrower cannot have more than $500 in outstanding payday loans.
Licensed lenders regulated under the Louisiana Consumer Credit Law.
LA.consumer_rights_summary: Louisiana allows payday lending with a $500 cap and tiered fee structure. The Office of Financial Institutions regulates all consumer lenders. Louisiana has one of the highest poverty rates in the nation, making consumer protections especially important. Complaints can be filed with OFI or the Attorney General.

credit_repair_laws.state_statute: Louisiana Credit Repair Services Organizations Act
key_provisions: Credit services organizations must provide written contract disclosing all services, fees, and cancellation rights before any payment is collected
Prohibition on charging upfront fees before services are performed; fees must be tied to actual results
Right to cancel contract within 3 business days without penalty or obligation
Credit repair companies must clearly identify themselves and disclose that consumers have the right to dispute inaccurate credit information themselves at no cost
Companies must maintain surety bond or letter of credit; false or misleading advertising is prohibited
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: Louisiana Office of Financial Institutions

personal_loans.rate_cap: 12% APR (default usury cap); higher rates allowed with written consent and compliance with specific exemptions
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans are governed under Louisiana Consumer Credit Law and general usury statutes. Lenders must be licensed with the Office of Financial Institutions.

payday_loans.rate_cap: $20 per $100 for loans up to $350; 16.75% per $100 for loans $350-$500
payday_loans.notes: Payday loans capped at $500 with tiered fee structure. Loan term must be 14-30 days. Borrowers cannot have more than $500 in outstanding payday loans at any time. Lenders must be licensed and report to the Louisiana Office of Financial Institutions. Rollovers are limited under state regulation.

title_loans.notes: Vehicle title loans are not explicitly authorized under Louisiana consumer lending statutes and are effectively prohibited. Motor vehicle liens are governed under commercial law but not as a primary consumer lending product.

installment_loans.rate_cap: Usury cap of 12% APR applies unless specific exemption is met; regulated lenders may charge higher rates with written agreement
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans are governed under the Louisiana Consumer Credit Law (La. Rev. Stat. § 9:3501 et seq.). Lenders must be licensed with the Office of Financial Institutions and must disclose all terms clearly.

mortgage.notes: Mortgages are regulated under Louisiana's Residential Mortgage Lending Law (La. Rev. Stat. § 6:961 et seq.). Mortgage lenders and loan officers must be licensed with the Office of Financial Institutions. Louisiana is a judicial foreclosure state, requiring court action and proper notice. High-cost mortgages are subject to additional disclosure and protections under HOEPA-equivalent standards.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 987) applies to all military members (active duty, National Guard, and Reserves) in Louisiana. It caps the Military Annual Percentage Rate (MAPR) at 36% for covered borrowers and requires clear disclosure of loan terms, cost, and payment schedule before agreement. All short-term and payday lenders in Louisiana must verify military status and comply with the 36% MAPR cap when applicable.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3951 et seq.) provides active duty service members in Louisiana with a 6% maximum interest rate cap on pre-service debts, protection from foreclosure and eviction without court order, right to terminate certain contracts (including auto leases), and extended statute of limitations. Creditors must reduce rates to 6% upon receipt of military orders and proper notice.
state_specific_protections: Louisiana recognizes military service credit for public employment and may offer state-specific benefits through the Louisiana Military Family Assistance program
Property tax exemptions available for disabled veterans and surviving spouses under La. Rev. Stat. § 47:4941
Louisiana Department of Veterans Affairs provides financial counseling and assistance programs for eligible veterans in financial distress
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: Louisiana participates in VA home loan programs administered by the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs. The state does not impose additional licensing requirements for VA-approved lenders beyond federal standards. Property transfer tax exemptions may apply for veterans purchasing homes with VA loans in certain jurisdictions.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Joint Base Barksdale-Bossier (Barksdale Air Force Base and Barksdale Army Airfield near Shreveport); Naval Station Pascagoula (adjacent to Louisiana border in Mississippi); Fort Polk (closed as of September 2023, but historically significant)

complaint_resources.name: Louisiana Office of Financial Institutions (OFI)
complaint_resources.description: Primary state regulator for consumer lenders, payday loan companies, credit services organizations, mortgage lenders, and loan officers. Handles licensing, examination, and complaint resolution for financial institutions.

complaint_resources.name: Louisiana Attorney General - Consumer Protection Section
complaint_resources.description: Enforces Louisiana Consumer Credit Law and credit services organization laws. Investigates consumer complaints regarding unfair, deceptive, or abusive lending practices and fraud.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Federal complaints regarding payday loans, personal loans, mortgages, credit reporting, and other consumer financial products. CFPB coordinates with state regulators.

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
complaint_resources.description: Complaints about credit repair scams, identity theft, and deceptive lending practices.

complaint_resources.name: Louisiana State Police - Financial Crimes Division
complaint_resources.description: Investigates financial fraud and predatory lending practices in Louisiana.

statute_links.name: Louisiana Consumer Credit Law
statute_links.description: Comprehensive regulation of consumer loans, including rate caps, licensing requirements, and consumer protections (La. Rev. Stat. § 9:3501 et seq.)

statute_links.name: Louisiana Payday Loan Statute
statute_links.description: Specific provisions governing payday loans, fee structures, term limits, and rollover restrictions (La. Rev. Stat. § 9:3501-3515)

statute_links.name: Louisiana Credit Repair Services Organizations Act
statute_links.description: Regulation of credit repair companies, including bonding requirements, fee restrictions, and consumer cancellation rights (La. Rev. Stat. § 9:3573.1 et seq.)

statute_links.name: Louisiana Residential Mortgage Lending Law
statute_links.description: Regulation of mortgage lenders, loan officers, and mortgage brokers; includes licensing and high-cost mortgage protections (La. Rev. Stat. § 6:961 et seq.)

statute_links.name: Louisiana Usury Statute
statute_links.description: Default 12% APR cap with exemptions for certain regulated lenders and specialized loan types (La. Rev. Stat. § 9:3501)

statute_links.name: Louisiana Office of Financial Institutions - Regulations
statute_links.description: Administrative rules and regulations for consumer lenders, payday loan companies, and credit services organizations

LA.recent_changes: Louisiana enacted amendments to the Consumer Credit Law in 2023 strengthening payday loan oversight and database tracking requirements to reduce debt traps. The Office of Financial Institutions implemented enhanced examination procedures for payday lenders in 2024 to ensure compliance with the $500 cap and fee-sharing rules.

ME.name: Maine
ME.usury_cap: 30% for supervised loans under $2,000; no specific payday loan statute
ME.consumer_protection_agency: Maine Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
ME.licensing_board: Maine Bureau of Consumer Credit Protection
key_regulations: No specific payday loan statute; lenders must comply with general usury and licensing laws.
Supervised lender rates capped at 30% for loans under $2,000.
The Maine Consumer Credit Code provides comprehensive consumer protections.
The Bureau of Consumer Credit Protection is the primary regulator for consumer lending.
ME.consumer_rights_summary: Maine does not have specific payday loan legislation but regulates consumer lending under its Consumer Credit Code with rate caps that make traditional payday lending impractical. The Bureau of Consumer Credit Protection enforces lending laws. Consumers can file complaints with the Bureau or the Attorney General.

credit_repair_laws.state_statute: Maine Consumer Credit Code — Loan Broker Provisions (Article 10)
credit_repair_laws.statute_code: Me. Rev. Stat. Ann. tit. 9-A, § 10-101 et seq.
key_provisions: Credit services organizations must provide written contract before any services are performed, clearly stating all charges and timeline for results
Prohibition on charging upfront fees before services are actually delivered
Requirement to disclose consumer's right to dispute items directly with credit bureaus at no cost
Prohibition on misrepresenting services, guaranteeing specific outcomes, or claiming to remove accurate negative information
Organizations must not require waiver of consumer rights or make services conditional on waiving dispute rights
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: Maine Bureau of Consumer Credit Protection

personal_loans.rate_cap: No specific cap for unsupervised loans; 30% APR for supervised loans under $2,000
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans are governed under Maine Consumer Credit Code. Rates depend on whether lender is licensed/supervised. Unsupervised lenders generally face no state rate cap but must comply with federal law.

payday_loans.rate_cap: Effectively prohibited; 30% usury cap on supervised loans makes traditional payday lending model impractical
payday_loans.notes: Maine has no specific payday loan statute. General usury laws and the 30% cap for supervised loans under $2,000 effectively restrict payday lending. Lenders offering payday-style products must comply with Consumer Credit Code and general usury limitations.

title_loans.notes: Title loans are prohibited in Maine. No specific statute allowing vehicle title lending as collateral for short-term loans.

installment_loans.rate_cap: No specific cap for unsupervised lenders; supervised lenders subject to 30% APR cap for loans under $2,000
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans are governed under Me. Rev. Stat. Ann. tit. 9-A (Maine Consumer Credit Code). Rates vary based on lender licensing status and loan amount.

mortgage.notes: Maine mortgages are governed under Me. Rev. Stat. Ann. tit. 9-A and supervised by the Maine Bureau of Consumer Credit Protection for residential mortgages. Foreclosure is judicial; lenders must file suit in court. High-cost home loans (HCML) defined as loans with APR 8% above prime or points/fees exceeding 8% of loan amount are subject to additional protections including escrow requirements and anti-prepayment penalty rules.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 987) establishes a 36% MAPR (Military Annual Percentage Rate) cap for covered borrowers on active duty and their dependents.
