Nevada payday lenders and other consumer lenders are subject to this 36% MAPR cap when lending to covered borrowers, notwithstanding Nevada's lack of a general rate cap.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3953) provides active-duty service members with a 6% interest rate cap on pre-service debts, prohibits foreclosure or eviction without court order, allows lease termination with 30-day notice in certain cases, and provides protection against default judgments. These protections apply in Nevada regardless of state law.
state_specific_protections: Nevada grants a property tax exemption for veterans with service-connected disabilities rated at 100% or 60% or more by the VA; surviving spouses of veterans may also qualify
Nevada recognizes active-duty military members for residency and tuition purposes; military members stationed in Nevada are considered state residents for higher education costs
Nevada has no state-specific military lending restrictions beyond federal MLA; however, the Financial Institutions Division enforces all applicable federal protections
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: Nevada does not impose state-level restrictions on VA loans. VA loans are federally guaranteed and subject to federal underwriting standards. Nevada recognizes VA Certificate of Eligibility; no state-specific VA loan programs exist, but Nevada housing authority may offer down payment assistance programs for eligible borrowers including veterans.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Nellis Air Force Base (Las Vegas area) is the major federal military installation in Nevada; Naval Air Station Fallon is also located in the state. These installations are primary focus areas for Military Lending Act enforcement.

complaint_resources.name: Nevada Financial Institutions Division
complaint_resources.description: Regulates payday lenders, installment lenders, credit repair companies, and other financial service providers under Nevada law; handles licensing, examinations, and consumer complaints

complaint_resources.name: Nevada Attorney General Bureau of Consumer Protection
complaint_resources.description: Investigates consumer complaints related to deceptive trade practices, payday lending violations, credit repair fraud, and other lending-related consumer abuses

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints about payday loans, personal loans, mortgages, credit reporting, and other consumer financial products; investigates violations of federal consumer protection laws

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
complaint_resources.description: Handles complaints related to credit repair scams, predatory lending, identity theft, and other deceptive practices

statute_links.name: Nevada Deferred Deposit Loan Law (Payday Loans)
statute_links.description: Nev. Rev. Stat. § 604A.200-604A.215; regulates payday loan licensing, loan amounts, terms, and lender conduct

statute_links.name: Nevada Credit Services Organization Act
statute_links.description: Nev. Rev. Stat. § 598.741-598.787; governs credit repair companies, bonding, registration, contract requirements, and consumer rights

statute_links.name: Nevada Consumer Lending Laws
statute_links.description: Nev. Rev. Stat. § 604A; umbrella statute covering consumer lending, installment loans, and licensing of lenders

statute_links.name: Nevada Mortgage and Deed of Trust Law
statute_links.description: Nev. Rev. Stat. § 107.010-107.480; regulates mortgages, deeds of trust, and non-judicial foreclosure procedures

statute_links.name: Nevada Truth in Lending Law
statute_links.description: Nev. Rev. Stat. § 599.010-599.150; state implementation and supplement to federal TILA disclosures

NV.recent_changes: Nevada has not enacted significant new consumer lending or credit repair legislation in 2024-2025. The regulatory framework remains largely stable, with ongoing oversight by the Financial Institutions Division and Attorney General under existing statutes.

NH.name: New Hampshire
NH.usury_cap: 36% APR cap on payday loans (2008); general usury cap tied to prime rate
NH.consumer_protection_agency: New Hampshire Attorney General Consumer Protection Bureau
key_regulations: Payday loans are capped at 36% APR under a 2008 law, effectively eliminating traditional payday lenders.
The general usury cap is linked to the prime rate plus a margin.
Licensed small loan companies regulated by the Banking Department.
The New Hampshire Consumer Protection Act provides strong anti-predatory lending protections.
NH.consumer_rights_summary: New Hampshire capped payday loan APR at 36% in 2008, effectively shutting down most payday lenders in the state. The Banking Department regulates all consumer lending, and the state's Consumer Protection Act provides additional safeguards. Complaints can be filed with the Banking Department or the Attorney General.

credit_repair_laws.state_statute: New Hampshire Credit Services Organization Act
key_provisions: Credit repair companies must provide written contract detailing services, fees, timeline, and consumer rights before accepting payment
Prohibition on upfront fees; companies may only charge after services are completed and results achieved
Companies must disclose that consumers have the right to dispute items on credit reports directly with credit bureaus at no cost
Right to cancel contract within 3 business days without penalty or obligation
Prohibition on making false claims about ability to remove accurate negative information from credit reports

personal_loans.rate_cap: Prime rate plus margin (general usury cap); specific rates determined by loan type and lender classification
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans from licensed lenders regulated by New Hampshire Banking Department. Rate cap tied to Federal Reserve prime rate.

payday_loans.rate_cap: 36% APR (2008 law effectively eliminates traditional payday lending)
payday_loans.notes: Payday loans effectively restricted by 36% APR cap enacted in 2008, which eliminated most traditional payday lenders in the state. Only alternative lenders operating under small loan company licenses may provide short-term loans.

title_loans.rate_cap: No title loan industry operating; subject to general usury caps
title_loans.notes: Title loans are not a significant or regulated lending product in New Hampshire. Subject to general usury regulations.

installment_loans.rate_cap: Prime rate plus margin (regulated by Banking Department for small loan companies); rates vary by lender license classification
installment_loans.notes: Licensed small loan companies in New Hampshire may offer installment loans. Governed by N.H. Rev. Stat. Ann. § 399-A (Small Loan Law) and regulated by the Banking Department.

mortgage.notes: New Hampshire mortgages are regulated under state real estate law and federal regulations (TRID, RESPA, TILA). Foreclosures are judicial (court-required process). State requires mortgage servicer licensing. Homeowner protection laws include right to cure and right to counsel under certain circumstances.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (36 MAPR cap) applies to service members and their dependents nationwide, including New Hampshire. The 36% MAPR cap includes interest, fees, and credit insurance charges on covered borrowing. Creditors must verify military status before applicable rates apply.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: Service members covered by the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (SCRA) receive a 6% interest rate cap on pre-service debts, protection from foreclosure and eviction during active duty, lease termination rights, and access to civil court protections. Applies to all service members with pre-service obligations.
state_specific_protections: New Hampshire recognizes military service as grounds for special civil relief; state courts must follow SCRA requirements for all service members regardless of state residency
Property tax exemptions available for surviving spouses and dependents of military personnel who died in service
New Hampshire veterans are eligible for VA mortgage loan programs with no down payment requirement on state-assessed properties
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: New Hampshire offers VA-backed mortgage loans with favorable terms through the New Hampshire Housing Finance Authority. Veterans may also qualify for property tax relief programs for surviving dependents.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Pease Air National Guard Base (Portsmouth area) is the primary military installation in New Hampshire. Residents near military areas should be aware of MLA protections when seeking consumer credit.

complaint_resources.name: New Hampshire Banking Department - Consumer Credit Division
complaint_resources.description: Handles complaints against licensed lenders, credit services organizations, and enforces consumer lending laws. Regulates payday lenders, small loan companies, and mortgage servicers.

complaint_resources.name: New Hampshire Attorney General - Consumer Protection Bureau
complaint_resources.description: Enforces consumer protection laws including the Consumer Protection Act. Handles complaints about unfair or deceptive lending practices and credit repair fraud.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Federal agency receiving complaints about consumer financial products and services, including payday loans, credit repair, mortgages, and credit cards.

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
complaint_resources.description: Handles complaints about credit repair scams, identity theft, and fraudulent lending practices.

statute_links.name: N.H. Rev. Stat. Ann. § 359-D (Credit Services Organization Act)
statute_links.description: State law regulating credit repair companies, requiring written contracts, prohibiting upfront fees, and providing consumer cancellation rights.

statute_links.name: N.H. Rev. Stat. Ann. § 399-A (Small Loan Law)
statute_links.description: Regulates small loan companies and consumer lending, including rate caps and licensing requirements administered by the Banking Department.

statute_links.name: N.H. Rev. Stat. Ann. § 21:34-a (Payday Loan Rate Cap)
statute_links.description: 2008 law capping payday loan APR at 36%, effectively eliminating traditional payday lending in New Hampshire.

statute_links.name: N.H. Rev. Stat. Ann. § 359:1 et seq. (Consumer Protection Act)
statute_links.description: Provides broad consumer protections against unfair and deceptive practices, including predatory lending and fraudulent credit services.

statute_links.name: N.H. Rev. Stat. Ann. § 21:33 (General Usury Law)
statute_links.description: Establishes general usury cap tied to Federal Reserve prime rate plus margin for unsecured consumer loans.

NH.recent_changes: No significant changes to consumer lending or credit repair laws in 2024-2025. The 36% APR payday loan cap enacted in 2008 remains the primary consumer protection measure limiting predatory short-term lending.

NJ.name: New Jersey
NJ.usury_cap: 30% for consumer loans (criminal usury); payday lending banned
NJ.consumer_protection_agency: New Jersey Attorney General Division of Consumer Affairs
NJ.licensing_board: New Jersey Department of Banking and Insurance
key_regulations: Payday lending is banned; New Jersey does not license payday lenders.
Criminal usury threshold is 30% for consumer loans.
Licensed consumer lenders under the New Jersey Licensed Lenders Act.
The New Jersey Consumer Fraud Act provides broad protections against deceptive lending.
NJ.consumer_rights_summary: New Jersey bans payday lending and maintains a 30% criminal usury threshold. The Consumer Fraud Act provides broad protections against predatory lending practices. Consumers can file complaints with the Division of Consumer Affairs or the Department of Banking and Insurance.

credit_repair_laws.state_statute: New Jersey Debt Adjustment and Credit Counseling Act
key_provisions: Credit repair organizations must provide written contracts detailing services, costs, timeline, and consumer rights before any services are performed
Prohibition on charging or collecting fees before services are fully delivered and results are achieved
Organizations must disclose that consumers have the right to dispute inaccurate credit report information directly with credit bureaus at no cost
Requirement to maintain surety bond of $10,000 and register with the New Jersey Department of Banking and Insurance
Consumer right to cancel contract within 3 business days without penalty
Prohibition on misrepresenting credit repair services or guaranteeing specific credit score improvements
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: New Jersey Department of Banking and Insurance

personal_loans.rate_cap: 30% APR (criminal usury threshold); civil usury cap traditionally 6% but subject to contract terms for licensed lenders
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans offered by licensed consumer lenders under the New Jersey Licensed Lenders Act are permitted. Rates above 30% constitute criminal usury for consumer loans.

payday_loans.rate_cap: Banned
payday_loans.notes: New Jersey has a comprehensive ban on payday lending. No payday lenders are licensed in the state. Violations can result in criminal charges.

title_loans.rate_cap: Not applicable (effectively banned under consumer protection statutes)
title_loans.notes: Title loans are not explicitly licensed or permitted in New Jersey. The 30% criminal usury cap and consumer protection laws effectively restrict such high-cost secured lending.

installment_loans.rate_cap: 30% APR criminal usury cap applies; licensed consumer lenders may charge rates up to this threshold
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans are permitted under the New Jersey Licensed Lenders Act. Lenders must be licensed by the Department of Banking and Insurance and comply with consumer protection requirements.

mortgage.notes: Mortgages in New Jersey are regulated under state and federal law. New Jersey uses judicial foreclosure. Lenders must be licensed or exempt. The state has protections including mortgage payment shock rules and requirements for loss mitigation review. Non-traditional mortgages and high-cost mortgages are subject to additional disclosure and licensing requirements under N.J.A.C. 3:20-1 et seq.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (36 MAPR cap) applies to active duty service members and their dependents. New Jersey lenders must comply with the 36% Maximum Annual Percentage Rate threshold, disclosure requirements, and prohibited terms (mandatory arbitration, wage assignment, vehicle disabling devices) under 10 U.S.C. § 987.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act provides a 6% interest cap on pre-service debts, protection from foreclosure and eviction during active duty, lease termination rights without penalty, and other protections. New Jersey courts enforce SCRA protections, and creditors must provide notices to service members.
state_specific_protections: New Jersey has a Property Tax Deduction for veterans with service-connected disabilities under N.J.S.A. 54:4-3.30
The state offers Veterans' Free Tuition Benefit for higher education through the Office of the Secretary of Higher Education
New Jersey provides foreclosure counseling and mediation services through HUD-approved counseling agencies, with special attention to military families
State law requires lenders to comply with federal SCRA notice requirements and provide clear disclosure of borrower rights
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: New Jersey participates in VA home loan guarantee programs. The New Jersey Housing and Mortgage Finance Agency (NJHMFA) offers programs that complement VA benefits. Property tax exemptions available for veterans with service-connected disabilities. No state-specific VA loan rate caps beyond federal requirements.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Joint Base McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst (Burlington County); Naval Weapons Station Earle (Monmouth County); Fort Monmouth (Monmouth County, closure status varies)

complaint_resources.name: New Jersey Department of Banking and Insurance - Consumer Complaints
complaint_resources.description: Handles complaints against licensed lenders, credit repair organizations, and other financial service providers regulated by DOBI

complaint_resources.name: New Jersey Attorney General - Division of Consumer Affairs
complaint_resources.description: Enforces the New Jersey Consumer Fraud Act and handles consumer complaints related to predatory lending, deceptive practices, and credit services fraud

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Federal agency handling complaints about consumer financial products including loans, credit reports, and credit services

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC) - Credit Repair
complaint_resources.description: Investigates credit repair fraud and illegal credit services organization practices

statute_links.name: New Jersey Licensed Lenders Act
statute_links.description: Establishes licensing requirements and rate limits for consumer lenders operating in New Jersey

statute_links.name: New Jersey Credit Services Organization Act (N.J.S.A. 17:16G-1 et seq.)
statute_links.description: Regulates credit repair organizations, requires bonding, registration, and prohibits upfront fees

statute_links.name: New Jersey Consumer Fraud Act (N.J.S.A. 56:8-1 et seq.)
statute_links.description: Provides broad consumer protections against deceptive and unfair lending practices

statute_links.name: New Jersey Usury Laws (N.J.S.A. 31:1-1 et seq.)
statute_links.description: Establishes 30% criminal usury threshold for consumer loans and defines illegal interest rates

statute_links.name: Payday Lending Prohibition
statute_links.description: New Jersey does not license or permit payday lending; violations are subject to criminal penalties

NJ.recent_changes: No significant changes to New Jersey's core lending and credit repair laws in 2024-2025. The state continues to maintain its comprehensive payday lending ban and 30% usury cap. Recent enforcement focus has remained on predatory lending practices and unlicensed lending operations.

NM.name: New Mexico
NM.usury_cap: 36% APR cap on all consumer loans (2023 law); payday lending effectively banned
NM.consumer_protection_agency: New Mexico Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
NM.licensing_board: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department - Financial Institutions Division
key_regulations: SB 66 (2023) capped all consumer loans at 36% APR including fees, effectively banning payday lending.
Prior to 2023, payday loans were allowed up to $2,500 with high fees.
Licensed small loan companies must comply with the new 36% cap.
The New Mexico Unfair Practices Act provides additional consumer protections.
NM.consumer_rights_summary: New Mexico enacted a 36% APR cap on all consumer loans in 2023, effectively banning payday lending. This was a significant reform given the state's high poverty rate. Consumers can file complaints with the Financial Institutions Division or the Attorney General's Consumer Protection Division.

credit_repair_laws.state_statute: New Mexico Credit Services Organization Act
key_provisions: Credit repair organizations must provide written contract clearly stating services, costs, timeline, and consumer rights before charging any fees
Prohibition on making false or misleading claims about ability to improve credit scores or remove negative information
Requirement to disclose that consumer has right to dispute items directly with credit bureaus without paying for services
Prohibition on charging fees until services are actually performed and consumer receives promised results
Must comply with the New Mexico Unfair Practices Act (§ 57-12-1 et seq.) regarding deceptive trade practices
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: New Mexico Attorney General Consumer Protection Division

personal_loans.rate_cap: 36% APR (all-in including fees)
personal_loans.notes: New Mexico SB 66 (2023) capped all consumer loans at 36% APR including all fees and charges. Licensed small loan companies must comply. No specific loan amount cap for personal loans.

payday_loans.rate_cap: Effectively banned (36% APR cap makes payday lending business model unviable)
payday_loans.notes: SB 66 (2023) effectively banned payday lending by capping all consumer loans at 36% APR including fees. Prior to 2023, payday loans were allowed up to $2,500 with high fee structures. Any payday lender operating in New Mexico must comply with the 36% APR cap or cease operations.

title_loans.rate_cap: Subject to 36% APR cap; business model effectively unviable
title_loans.notes: Title lending is not explicitly banned but the 36% APR cap (SB 66, 2023) effectively prohibits the traditional high-fee title loan business model in New Mexico.

installment_loans.rate_cap: 36% APR (all-in including all fees and charges)
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans are legal and governed by the 36% APR cap under SB 66 (2023). Licensed small loan companies and installment lenders must comply with the rate cap. Governed by N.M. Stat. Ann. § 58-5-1 et seq. (Small Loan Act, as amended by SB 66).

mortgage.notes: New Mexico mortgages are regulated under N.M. Stat. Ann. § 48-7-1 et seq. (Mortgage Loan Originator License Act). Foreclosure is non-judicial (power of sale) in New Mexico; lenders must provide notice and opportunity to cure. Judicial foreclosure is also available. Homestead exemption available for primary residences up to $30,000 in equity (N.M. Stat. Ann. § 42-10-9). Licensing required for mortgage originators through the Financial Institutions Division.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 987) applies to active duty service members in New Mexico, capping interest rates at 36% MAPR (Military Annual Percentage Rate, which includes all fees and charges). Covered borrowers receive protections including mandatory waiting periods, limits on mandatory arbitration, and prohibition of certain default fees.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3951 et seq.) provides pre-service debt protection capping interest at 6%, protections against foreclosure and eviction, lease termination rights, and stay of proceedings. SCRA applies to active duty service members with debts incurred before military service.
state_specific_protections: New Mexico Property Tax Code (§ 7-4-8) provides full property tax exemption for disabled veterans with service-connected disabilities rated at 100%
New Mexico Veterans' Services Department administers state benefits and can provide information on veteran-specific lending protections and loan programs
The state's 36% APR cap (SB 66, 2023) provides baseline consumer protection that exceeds MLA requirements for all residents including active duty military and veterans
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: New Mexico Housing Finance Authority administers VA loan programs and housing assistance. State offers property tax exemptions for veterans: disabled veterans (50% or more disability rating) receive exemption on up to $200,000 of home value. VA loans in New Mexico are subject to standard underwriting and state mortgage regulations (N.M. Stat. Ann. § 48-7-1 et seq.). No state-specific VA loan rate caps beyond federal regulations.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Kirtland Air Force Base (Albuquerque) is a major military installation in New Mexico. Holloman Air Force Base located in Alamogordo, New Mexico also has significant military population.

complaint_resources.name: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department - Financial Institutions Division
complaint_resources.description: Handles licensing and regulation of mortgage lenders, small loan companies, and other financial institutions. Accepts complaints regarding violations of lending laws and licensing requirements.

complaint_resources.name: New Mexico Attorney General - Consumer Protection Division
complaint_resources.description: Enforces consumer protection laws including the New Mexico Unfair Practices Act and credit repair laws. Accepts complaints about unlawful lending practices, credit repair fraud, and deceptive business practices.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Federal agency that accepts complaints about consumer financial products and services including lending and credit practices. Complaints are shared with companies and state regulators.

complaint_resources.name: New Mexico Better Business Bureau
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints about lending and credit-related businesses; provides dispute resolution and tracks complaint history of businesses.

statute_links.name: SB 66 (2023) - Consumer Loan Rate Cap
statute_links.description: Senate Bill 66 (2023) amended the Small Loan Act to cap all consumer loans at 36% APR including all fees and charges, effectively banning payday and title lending in New Mexico.

statute_links.name: New Mexico Small Loan Act (N.M. Stat. Ann. § 58-5-1 et seq.)
statute_links.description: Governs small loans and installment lending; amended by SB 66 to include 36% APR cap and compliance requirements for licensed lenders.

statute_links.name: New Mexico Credit Services Organization Act (N.M. Stat. Ann. § 57-3A-1 to 57-3A-10)
statute_links.description: Regulates credit repair companies; requires written contracts, prohibits upfront fees, prohibits deceptive claims, and provides consumers with 3-day cancellation rights.

statute_links.name: New Mexico Unfair Practices Act (N.M. Stat. Ann. § 57-12-1 et seq.)
statute_links.description: Prohibits deceptive trade practices and unfair methods of competition affecting consumers; applies to lending and credit services.

statute_links.name: New Mexico Mortgage Loan Originator License Act (N.M. Stat. Ann. § 48-7-1 et seq.)
statute_links.description: Requires licensing for mortgage loan originators and establishes regulatory requirements and consumer protections for mortgage lending.

statute_links.name: New Mexico Homestead Exemption (N.M. Stat. Ann. § 42-10-9)
statute_links.description: Provides property tax exemption on homestead property up to $30,000 in equity value for qualifying primary residences.

NM.recent_changes: Senate Bill 66 (2023) represents the most significant recent change to New Mexico consumer lending law, capping all consumer loans at 36% APR including fees and effectively banning payday lending. This law became effective and continues to reshape the state's lending landscape by eliminating high-cost short-term borrowing options.

NY.name: New York
NY.usury_cap: 16% civil usury; 25% criminal usury; payday lending banned
NY.consumer_protection_agency: New York Attorney General Consumer Frauds Bureau
NY.licensing_board: New York Department of Financial Services
key_regulations: Payday lending is banned; civil usury cap of 16% and criminal usury cap of 25% make it illegal.
The Department of Financial Services actively enforces against online payday lenders targeting NY residents.
Licensed lenders under the Banking Law may charge rates agreed upon for certain loan types.
The NY Consumer Protection Act and General Business Law Article 22-A protect consumers.
NY.consumer_rights_summary: New York bans payday lending through its 16% civil usury and 25% criminal usury caps. The Department of Financial Services aggressively pursues illegal online payday lenders. Consumers have strong protections under state law and can file complaints with DFS or the Attorney General.

credit_repair_laws.state_statute: New York Credit Services Business Act
credit_repair_laws.statute_code: N.Y. Gen. Bus. Law Article 28-BB, §§ 458-a through 458-k
key_provisions: Credit services organizations must provide written disclosures before any contract is signed, including a statement of the consumer's right to cancel within 3 business days
Prohibited from charging or collecting fees before delivering promised services to the consumer
Cannot make false or misleading claims about ability to improve credit records or remove accurate negative information
Must provide a detailed written contract specifying the services to be performed, costs, and timeline for results
Prohibited from advising consumers to refuse or omit accurate information from credit applications
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: New York Department of Financial Services

personal_loans.rate_cap: 16% civil usury cap; licensed lenders may negotiate rates for certain loan types
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans from licensed lenders are permitted. Unlicensed lenders are subject to the 16% civil usury cap unless a specific exemption applies. Rates above 16% are civil usury; rates above 25% are criminal usury.

payday_loans.rate_cap: Banned via 16% civil usury and 25% criminal usury caps
payday_loans.notes: Payday lending is prohibited in New York. The 16% civil usury cap and 25% criminal usury cap effectively ban high-fee, short-term lending. The New York Department of Financial Services actively pursues illegal online payday lenders targeting NY residents.

title_loans.rate_cap: Subject to 16% civil usury cap and 25% criminal usury cap
title_loans.notes: Title loans are not explicitly regulated in New York statute but are generally not offered due to usury caps. Any secured lending would be subject to the 16% civil usury cap for unlicensed lenders.

installment_loans.rate_cap: 16% civil usury cap for unlicensed lenders; licensed lenders may negotiate rates under Banking Law
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans are legal in New York. Licensed lenders have greater flexibility in rate negotiation; unlicensed lenders are subject to the 16% civil usury cap. Consumer Protection Act (Gen. Bus. Law Article 22-A) requires clear disclosure of all terms.

mortgage.notes: New York mortgages are subject to judicial foreclosure requirements. Mortgages must comply with the Truth in Lending Act (TILA) and Regulation Z at the federal level. The New York Department of Financial Services supervises mortgage lenders and servicers. Homeowners have strong protections under the Foreclosure Prevention Act and must be offered loss mitigation options before foreclosure. A court judgment is required before foreclosure sale.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 987) establishes a 36% Military Annual Percentage Rate (MAPR) cap for covered borrowers (active duty service members and their covered dependents) on consumer credit products. The cap applies to payday loans, title loans, installment loans, and lines of credit but excludes mortgage loans, vehicle purchase loans, and credit cards. New York's stricter 16% civil usury cap supersedes the federal 36% MAPR cap for all consumers in the state.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3953) provides active duty service members with a 6% interest rate cap on pre-service debts, protection from foreclosure and eviction without court orders, and lease termination rights. SCRA applies nationwide and protects eligible service members from creditor actions while on active duty.
state_specific_protections: New York's 16% civil usury cap provides stronger protection than the federal 36% MAPR cap, automatically protecting all active duty service members in the state from predatory lending at rates above 16%
The New York Department of Financial Services actively investigates and prosecutes violations of lending laws targeting military members
Veterans and active duty service members can access free legal aid through New York's military legal assistance programs and pro bono legal services
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: New York recognizes VA loans as exempt from certain state usury restrictions under federal law. The state does not impose additional property tax exemptions beyond federal VA benefits, but veterans should verify eligibility with their county assessor. New York Housing Finance Agency offers information on VA loan programs and refinancing options.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Fort Drum (near Watertown, northern NY) is the major active military installation. Naval Station Newport is located in neighboring Rhode Island but serves NY personnel. Fort Hamilton in Brooklyn houses Army Reserve and National Guard units.

complaint_resources.name: New York Department of Financial Services
complaint_resources.description: Regulates and licenses lending institutions, credit repair organizations, and mortgage lenders. Handles complaints regarding illegal lending practices, usury violations, payday loan enforcement, and unlicensed lenders.

complaint_resources.name: New York Attorney General Consumer Frauds Bureau
complaint_resources.description: Enforces consumer protection laws including the Consumer Protection Act and General Business Law Article 22-A. Accepts complaints about lending fraud, unlicensed lenders, and deceptive credit repair practices.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints about consumer financial products and services including loans, credit reporting, and lending discrimination.

complaint_resources.name: Better Business Bureau - New York
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints about lending and credit services and provides dispute resolution.

statute_links.name: New York General Business Law Article 22-A (Consumer Protection Act)
statute_links.description: Comprehensive consumer protection statute prohibiting deceptive and unconscionable acts in consumer transactions, applies to all lending.

statute_links.name: N.Y. Gen. Bus. Law Article 28-BB (Credit Services Business Act)
statute_links.description: Regulates credit repair companies, requires bonding, registration, upfront fee prohibitions, and cancellation rights.

statute_links.name: New York Penal Law § 190 (Usury)
statute_links.description: Defines civil usury (16% cap) and criminal usury (25% cap); provides criminal penalties for violations.

statute_links.name: New York Banking Law § 4-a (Licensing of Lenders)
statute_links.description: Governs licensing of lenders and specifies rate negotiation powers for licensed financial institutions.

statute_links.name: New York Foreclosure Prevention Act
statute_links.description: Requires loss mitigation options and judicial foreclosure procedures for residential mortgages.

NY.recent_changes: In February 2026, Governor Hochul announced nation-leading rules requiring Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) providers to comply with the 16% interest cap (or 24.99% for DFS-licensed lenders), with maximum $8 late fees, licensing, disclosure, and data privacy requirements. Pending legislation (S3925, S1726) targets foreign banking corporations and earned wage access companies used to circumvent payday loan bans.

NC.name: North Carolina
NC.usury_cap: 8% general; 30% for consumer finance loans under $10,000; payday lending banned since 2001
NC.consumer_protection_agency: North Carolina Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
key_regulations: Payday lending banned since 2001 when the Check Cashers Act authorization expired.
Consumer finance companies limited to 30% APR on loans under $10,000.
The North Carolina Consumer Finance Act regulates all licensed consumer lending.
NC was one of the first states to ban payday lending, setting a national precedent.
NC.consumer_rights_summary: North Carolina banned payday lending in 2001, becoming one of the first states to do so. Consumer finance companies are regulated with rate caps. Consumers can file complaints with the Commissioner of Banks or the Attorney General's Consumer Protection Division.

credit_repair_laws.state_statute: North Carolina Credit Services Organization Act
key_provisions: Credit services organizations must provide consumers with a written contract before any services are rendered, detailing all charges and the timeline for results
Prohibition on misrepresenting the credit repair company's ability to improve credit reports or scores
Companies cannot charge or collect any fees before services are fully performed
Consumers have the right to cancel the contract without penalty within 5 business days of signing
Credit repair companies must maintain a surety bond of at least $25,000

personal_loans.rate_cap: 8% APR general usury cap; 30% APR for consumer finance companies on loans under $10,000
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans are regulated under North Carolina's general usury laws and the Consumer Finance Act. Licensed consumer finance companies may charge up to 30% APR on loans under $10,000.

payday_loans.rate_cap: Prohibited
payday_loans.notes: North Carolina banned payday lending in 2001 when the Check Cashers Act authorization expired. NC was one of the first states to implement a statewide payday loan ban, setting a national precedent.

title_loans.notes: Title loans are not explicitly addressed in statute but fall under the general prohibition on high-cost consumer lending. No licensed title loan industry operates in NC.

installment_loans.rate_cap: 8% APR general usury cap; 30% APR for consumer finance companies on loans under $10,000 under the Consumer Finance Act
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans are regulated under N.C. Gen. Stat. § 53-180 et seq. (Consumer Finance Act). Licensed lenders must comply with rate caps and truth-in-lending disclosures.

mortgage.notes: North Carolina mortgages are regulated under N.C. Gen. Stat. § 45-21.1 et seq. (Residential Mortgage Lending Act). Foreclosures proceed non-judicially when a power of sale is present in the deed of trust; otherwise judicial foreclosure is required. All mortgage servicers and brokers must be licensed by the Commissioner of Banks. North Carolina requires compliance with federal TILA and RESPA requirements.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The federal Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 987) caps the annual percentage rate (MAPR) at 36% for covered borrowers on active duty and their dependents. This applies to all consumer credit transactions in North Carolina. Covered borrowers include active duty service members, National Guard members on active duty, and their dependents.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3701 et seq.) provides protections including: 6% interest rate cap on pre-service debts; protection from foreclosure, eviction, and property replevin during active duty service; lease termination rights; and statute of limitations extensions. These federal protections apply automatically in North Carolina to eligible service members.
state_specific_protections: North Carolina provides property tax exemptions for disabled veterans and the surviving spouses/unremarried widows of veterans who died in service or from service-connected disabilities
The North Carolina Housing Finance Agency offers down payment assistance and favorable loan terms through the Veterans Homeownership Loan Program for eligible veterans
North Carolina law prohibits predatory lending practices specifically targeting military families and service members, enforced through the Attorney General's Consumer Protection Division
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: North Carolina has a robust VA loan market with the North Carolina Housing Finance Agency providing supplemental financing programs for VA-eligible borrowers. Veterans are eligible for property tax homestead exemptions ($25,000 exemption for disabled veterans rated 30% or higher by the VA). No state-level restrictions on VA loan terms exist beyond federal requirements.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Major military installations in North Carolina include: Fort Liberty (Fort Bragg) in Cumberland County (largest Army installation); Marine Corps Base Camp Lejeune in Onslow County; Naval Station Norfolk operations; Seymour Johnson Air Force Base in Wayne County; and Marine Corps Air Station Cherry Point in Carteret County.

complaint_resources.name: North Carolina Commissioner of Banks
complaint_resources.description: Regulates banks, credit unions, mortgage lenders, consumer finance companies, and credit services organizations. Handles complaints about licensed lenders and financial institutions.

complaint_resources.name: North Carolina Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
complaint_resources.description: Enforces consumer protection laws, including credit repair fraud, unfair/deceptive trade practices, and lending violations. Accepts consumer complaints about lending and credit services.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Federal agency that handles complaints about consumer financial products and services, including payday lenders, mortgage servicers, debt collectors, and credit reporting agencies.

complaint_resources.name: North Carolina Department of Justice - Consumer Complaint Hotline
complaint_resources.description: Direct complaint filing portal for North Carolina consumers experiencing unfair or deceptive trade practices in lending, credit repair, and consumer finance.

statute_links.name: North Carolina Consumer Finance Act
statute_links.description: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 53-180 et seq. Regulates consumer finance companies, installment lenders, and sets rate caps at 30% APR for loans under $10,000.

statute_links.name: North Carolina Credit Services Organization Act
statute_links.description: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 66-220 to 66-226. Regulates credit repair companies, requires written contracts, prohibits upfront fees, and mandates surety bonds.

statute_links.name: North Carolina General Usury Laws
statute_links.description: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 24-1 et seq. Establishes the 8% general usury cap on interest rates for most consumer transactions.

statute_links.name: North Carolina Residential Mortgage Lending Act
statute_links.description: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 53-243.01 et seq. Regulates residential mortgage lenders, servicers, and brokers. Requires licensing through the Commissioner of Banks.

statute_links.name: North Carolina Check Cashers Act (Expired 2001)
statute_links.description: Historical reference: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 53-288 et seq. This act authorized payday lending until its expiration in 2001, after which payday lending became prohibited in North Carolina.

statute_links.name: North Carolina Foreclosure and Deed of Trust Laws
statute_links.description: N.C. Gen. Stat. § 45-21.1 et seq. Governs non-judicial and judicial foreclosure procedures, requiring specific notice and acceleration provisions.

NC.recent_changes: No significant changes to core lending or credit repair statutes in 2024-2025. North Carolina continues enforcement of its 2001 payday loan ban and maintains the 30% APR cap for consumer finance companies under $10,000. Recent regulatory focus has been on mortgage servicing compliance and predatory lending enforcement.

ND.name: North Dakota
ND.usury_cap: 6% default rate; payday loans legal with $20 per $100 fee cap ($500 max loan)
ND.consumer_protection_agency: North Dakota Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
ND.licensing_board: North Dakota Department of Financial Institutions
key_regulations: Payday loans capped at $500 with maximum fee of $20 per $100 borrowed.
Maximum loan term is 60 days.
Rollovers are allowed but lender must offer a payment plan after extended use.
Licensed money brokers regulated under the North Dakota Deferred Deposit Service Act.
ND.consumer_rights_summary: North Dakota allows payday lending with a $500 cap and $20 per $100 fee limit. The state requires lenders to offer payment plans for borrowers who cannot repay. Complaints can be filed with the Department of Financial Institutions or the Attorney General.

credit_repair_laws.state_statute: North Dakota Credit Services Organization Act
key_provisions: Credit repair organizations must provide a written contract clearly disclosing all terms, conditions, and the consumer's cancellation rights before any payment is accepted
Prohibits claiming ability to remove accurate negative credit information or obtain credit for consumers with poor credit histories
Requires organizations to provide written notice of consumer rights under the Fair Credit Reporting Act and state law
Organizations must maintain escrow accounts for consumer funds until promised services are substantially completed
Prohibits requiring payment until promised services have been substantially completed
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: North Dakota Department of Financial Institutions

personal_loans.rate_cap: 6% default rate (N.D. Cent. Code § 47-14-08); rates above 6% allowed with written agreement
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans governed by North Dakota general usury law; rates above the default 6% cap require written agreement

payday_loans.rate_cap: $20 per $100 borrowed (equivalent to 520% APR on a 14-day loan)
payday_loans.notes: Regulated under North Dakota Deferred Deposit Service Act (N.D. Cent. Code § 13-08-01 to 13-08-11); lenders must offer payment plan after 60 days or extended use; rollovers allowed; licensed money brokers required

title_loans.notes: North Dakota does not permit vehicle title loans as a distinct lending product

installment_loans.rate_cap: 6% default rate unless parties agree otherwise in writing (N.D. Cent. Code § 47-14-08)
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans governed by North Dakota general usury and consumer credit laws; rates above 6% allowed with written agreement; subject to open-end and closed-end credit disclosure requirements

mortgage.notes: North Dakota mortgages governed by N.D. Cent. Code § 47-10 and related chapters; foreclosures are judicial proceedings; lender must file action in district court; right to cure exists prior to foreclosure; homestead exemption available; licensed mortgage lenders and servicers regulated by North Dakota Department of Financial Institutions

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 987) applies to active-duty service members and their dependents in North Dakota, capping Military Annual Percentage Rate (MAPR) at 36% for most consumer credit, including payday loans, title loans, and installment loans. Lenders must provide required disclosures and cannot enforce certain penalty provisions. Service members can file complaints with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau or with the Military Lending Act enforcement team.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3953) provides active-duty service members in North Dakota with a 6% interest rate cap on pre-service debts, protection against default judgments and foreclosures without written consent or court order, and lease termination rights for military-related relocation. Benefits apply to service members on active duty and their dependents.
state_specific_protections: North Dakota recognizes the federal Military Lending Act requirements and enforces them through the North Dakota Department of Financial Institutions and Attorney General's Office
Payday lending for active-duty military members is subject to both the $20 per $100 state fee cap and the 36% MAPR federal cap, whichever is more restrictive
Service members may file complaints regarding MLA or SCRA violations with the North Dakota Attorney General Consumer Protection Division
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: North Dakota allows VA-guaranteed loans for eligible veterans and service members through federally approved lenders; state does not impose additional usury restrictions on VA loans beyond federal requirements; North Dakota provides property tax exemptions for disabled veterans (minimum 10% disability rating) on their primary residence; information available through the North Dakota Veterans' Benefits Office
veteran_protections.military_installations: Minot Air Force Base (largest military installation in North Dakota, located in Minot); Grand Forks Air Force Base (Grand Forks); both are subject to Military Lending Act enforcement and oversight by the North Dakota Department of Financial Institutions

complaint_resources.name: North Dakota Department of Financial Institutions
complaint_resources.description: Regulates banks, credit unions, money brokers, licensed lenders, mortgage companies, and payday loan providers; handles complaints regarding lending violations, payday loan disputes, and credit services organizations

complaint_resources.name: North Dakota Attorney General - Consumer Protection Division
complaint_resources.description: Investigates consumer complaints related to deceptive practices, fraud, and violations of consumer protection laws; handles payday loan complaints, credit repair organization disputes, and unlicensed lending

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints about payday loans, personal loans, mortgages, credit reporting, and Military Lending Act violations; accessible online and provides regular complaint data to state regulators

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints regarding credit repair organizations, identity theft, and other consumer fraud; shares data with law enforcement and state authorities

statute_links.name: North Dakota Deferred Deposit Service Act
statute_links.description: Primary statute governing payday loans in North Dakota; establishes $500 cap, $20 per $100 fee cap, 60-day maximum term, and payment plan requirements

statute_links.name: North Dakota Credit Services Organization Act (N.D. Cent. Code § 13-06-01 to 13-06-15)
statute_links.description: Regulates credit repair organizations; requires written contracts, prohibits upfront fees, establishes 5-day cancellation rights, and requires escrow accounts

statute_links.name: North Dakota General Usury Law (N.D. Cent. Code § 47-14-08)
statute_links.description: Establishes default 6% interest rate cap for loans without written agreement; allows rates above 6% with written agreement

statute_links.name: North Dakota Consumer Credit Code (N.D. Cent. Code § 34-01-01 et seq.)
statute_links.description: Comprehensive consumer credit regulations including disclosure requirements, financing charges, and creditor remedies

statute_links.name: North Dakota Mortgage Foreclosure Law (N.D. Cent. Code § 47-10)
statute_links.description: Governs judicial foreclosure procedures; requires lender to file court action; establishes borrower rights to cure and right to redemption

ND.recent_changes: No significant changes to North Dakota consumer lending or credit repair legislation in 2024-2025. The payday loan framework established under the Deferred Deposit Service Act remains unchanged with the $500 cap and $20 per $100 fee structure, though the Department of Financial Institutions continues enforcement activities regarding compliance with payment plan requirements and unlicensed lending.

OH.name: Ohio
OH.usury_cap: 28% APR cap on short-term loans (HB 123, 2018); 8% general usury
OH.consumer_protection_agency: Ohio Attorney General Consumer Protection Section
OH.licensing_board: Ohio Department of Commerce Division of Financial Institutions
key_regulations: HB 123 (2018) reformed payday lending with 28% APR cap plus a monthly maintenance fee.
Short-term loans capped at $1,000 with minimum term of 91 days.
Monthly maintenance fee of up to 10% of original principal (max $30/month).
A statewide database tracks borrower loans to prevent excessive lending.
OH.consumer_rights_summary: Ohio reformed payday lending in 2018 with HB 123, capping APR at 28% and requiring minimum 91-day terms. A statewide database prevents borrower abuse. The Division of Financial Institutions regulates consumer lenders, and consumers can file complaints with the Division or the Attorney General.

key_provisions: Credit repair organizations must provide a written contract before performing any services, detailing all terms, conditions, and charges
Companies must disclose in writing the consumer's right to dispute inaccurate items on their credit report directly with credit reporting agencies
Credit repair companies are prohibited from charging fees before services are actually performed or results are delivered
Contracts must include a written notice that credit repair organizations cannot remove accurate negative information from credit reports
Consumers have the right to cancel contracts within 3 business days without penalty or obligation
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: Ohio Attorney General Consumer Protection Section (oversight only, no formal registration)

personal_loans.rate_cap: 8% APR general usury cap applies unless exempt
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans are regulated under Ohio's general usury laws. Lenders must be licensed by the Ohio Department of Commerce Division of Financial Institutions. The 8% usury cap applies to most personal loans unless specific exemptions apply (such as licensed credit unions or certain institutional lenders).

payday_loans.rate_cap: 28% APR maximum on short-term loans under HB 123 (2018); monthly maintenance fee up to 10% of original principal (maximum $30/month)
payday_loans.notes: HB 123 (2018) substantially reformed Ohio payday lending. Short-term loans are capped at $1,000 with a minimum term of 91 days. Borrowers may have only one outstanding payday loan at a time. The Ohio Payday Loan Database (maintained by the Department of Commerce Division of Financial Institutions) tracks all payday loans to prevent excessive lending and debt traps. Lenders must verify borrower information in the database before issuing a loan.

title_loans.notes: Title loans (loans secured by vehicle titles) are prohibited in Ohio.

installment_loans.rate_cap: 8% APR general usury cap applies unless exempt; consumer finance lenders may operate under different rate structures with proper licensing
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans are permitted in Ohio and regulated by the Ohio Department of Commerce Division of Financial Institutions. Licensed consumer finance lenders may charge rates above the general usury cap under Ohio Rev. Code § 1321.01 et seq., provided they comply with licensing requirements and disclosure obligations.

mortgage.notes: Mortgages in Ohio are regulated under Ohio Rev. Code § 1322.01 et seq. (SCRA compliance), Ohio Rev. Code § 1321.01 et seq. (consumer finance), and federal mortgage regulations. Ohio uses non-judicial foreclosure through power of sale, though judicial foreclosure is also available. Lenders must comply with federal Truth in Lending Act (TILA), Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA), and Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA). The Ohio Attorney General and the Department of Commerce Division of Financial Institutions oversee mortgage lending practices. Foreclosure notices and timelines are governed by state statute.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The federal Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 987) applies to all active duty service members. The act caps the annual percentage rate (MAPR) at 36% on covered borrowing, including payday loans, vehicle title loans, installment loans, and deposit advance loans. It prohibits mandatory arbitration and certain loan terms that negatively affect service members. The MAPR cap applies to covered borrowers regardless of state law, meaning Ohio's 28% payday loan APR cap provides greater protection than the MLA's 36% cap.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3101 et seq.) provides active duty service members with a 6% interest rate cap on pre-service debts, including credit cards and personal loans incurred before active duty service. SCRA also provides protections against foreclosure, eviction, and lease termination; requires creditors to reduce interest rates and waive penalties; and mandates that service members must provide written notice of active duty status to creditors. Service members must proactively notify creditors of their status to receive protections.
state_specific_protections: Ohio Rev. Code § 5923.01 provides a property tax exemption for certain disabled veterans and their surviving spouses
Ohio offers military family leave protections under Ohio Admin. Code § 4160-2-01 for employees called to active duty
The Ohio Veterans Disability and Housing Assistance Program provides loans and grants to eligible veterans for home purchases and adaptations
The Ohio Department of Veterans Services administers benefits and connects veterans with available state and federal resources, including credit counseling referrals
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: Ohio recognizes VA loans (guaranteed by the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs) and has no state-imposed restrictions on VA loan terms beyond federal requirements. Veterans may apply for VA-guaranteed mortgages through participating lenders in Ohio. The state does not impose additional licensing requirements on VA lenders beyond federal standards.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Major military installations in Ohio include: Wright-Patterson Air Force Base (Dayton), Naval Station Recruitment Command (Lorain), and the Ohio Army National Guard and Air National Guard facilities throughout the state. These installations are priority enforcement areas for Military Lending Act compliance.

complaint_resources.name: Ohio Department of Commerce Division of Financial Institutions
complaint_resources.description: Regulates consumer lenders, payday loan lenders, and mortgage lenders in Ohio. Investigates complaints about unfair or deceptive lending practices, licensing violations, and violations of the payday loan reform law (HB 123). Maintains the Ohio Payday Loan Database.

complaint_resources.name: Ohio Attorney General Consumer Protection Section
complaint_resources.description: Handles consumer complaints regarding unfair, deceptive, or abusive lending practices; credit repair fraud; and violations of Ohio consumer protection laws. Can pursue enforcement actions against lenders and credit repair companies engaging in illegal conduct.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints about payday loans, title loans, personal loans, mortgages, credit cards, and other consumer financial products. Investigates potential violations of federal consumer financial protection laws including the Truth in Lending Act, Dodd-Frank Act, and Military Lending Act.

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
complaint_resources.description: Handles complaints about credit repair fraud, identity theft, and deceptive lending practices. Investigates violations of the Credit Repair Organizations Act and other federal consumer protection statutes.

complaint_resources.name: Better Business Bureau - Ohio
complaint_resources.description: Provides consumer complaint resolution services for lending and credit-related disputes. While not a regulatory agency, the BBB maintains complaint records and business ratings that inform consumers and regulators.

statute_links.name: Ohio Rev. Code § 1321.01 et seq. (Consumer Finance Law)
statute_links.description: Governs consumer finance companies, small loan companies, and licensed lenders. Includes usury rate limits, licensing requirements, and disclosure obligations.

statute_links.name: Ohio Rev. Code § 1322.01 et seq. (Mortgage Lending)
statute_links.description: Regulates mortgage lenders and brokers, including licensing, disclosures, and foreclosure procedures. Implements federal mortgage lending requirements under TILA and RESPA.

statute_links.name: Ohio Rev. Code § 4712.01-4712.10 (Credit Services Organization Act)
statute_links.description: Regulates credit repair companies. Prohibits upfront fees, requires written contracts, mandates 3-day cancellation rights, and requires disclosure of consumer rights to dispute credit reports.

statute_links.name: HB 123 (2018) - Payday Lending Reform Law
statute_links.description: Reformed Ohio payday lending effective January 1, 2019. Capped APR at 28% on short-term loans, imposed 91-day minimum terms, set $1,000 maximum loan amount, limited borrowers to one outstanding payday loan, and created the Ohio Payday Loan Database.

statute_links.name: Ohio Rev. Code § 1349.01 et seq. (Payday Loan Database)
statute_links.description: Establishes the statewide Ohio Payday Loan Database that tracks all short-term loans to prevent excessive borrowing and predatory lending practices.

statute_links.name: Ohio Rev. Code § 5923.01 (Disabled Veterans Property Tax Exemption)
statute_links.description: Provides property tax exemptions for certain disabled veterans and their surviving spouses, reducing housing costs for eligible veterans.

statute_links.name: Ohio Administrative Code 4160-2-01 (Military Family Leave)
statute_links.description: Establishes military family leave protections for employees called to active military duty.

OH.recent_changes: No significant changes to Ohio consumer lending or credit repair laws in 2024-2025. The most recent major reform was HB 123 (2018), which substantially reformed payday lending and remains the governing law. The Ohio Payday Loan Database continues to operate effectively in preventing excessive lending.

OK.name: Oklahoma
OK.usury_cap: 6% default rate; payday loans capped at $500 with $15 per $100 fee for first $300
OK.consumer_protection_agency: Oklahoma Attorney General Consumer Protection Unit
key_regulations: Payday loans (deferred deposit lending) capped at $500 with tiered fees: $15 per $100 on first $300, $10 per $100 on balance.
Maximum loan term is 12-45 days.
Borrowers may not have more than two outstanding payday loans at once.
The Oklahoma Deferred Deposit Lending Act regulates all payday lenders.
OK.consumer_rights_summary: Oklahoma allows payday lending with a $500 cap and tiered fee structure. Borrowers are limited to two outstanding loans at a time. The Department of Consumer Credit regulates lenders, and complaints can be filed with the Department or the Attorney General.

key_provisions: Credit repair companies must provide written contract with detailed services, costs, and timeline before any work begins
Companies must inform consumers of their right to dispute inaccurate credit information directly with credit bureaus at no cost
Companies cannot charge upfront fees; fees must be tied to actual services delivered
Companies cannot make false or misleading statements about their ability to improve credit scores or remove accurate negative information
Consumers have right to cancel services within 3 business days without penalty
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: Oklahoma Department of Consumer Credit

personal_loans.rate_cap: 6% default usury rate (Okla. Stat. tit. 15, § 266); however, lenders may contract for higher rates if loan agreement specifies rate
personal_loans.notes: Personal loans regulated under general Oklahoma usury law; specific rate caps may apply by lender type. Installment loans and consumer finance company loans may have different rate structures under Oklahoma Consumer Finance Act.

payday_loans.rate_cap: $15 per $100 borrowed on first $300 of loan; $10 per $100 on remaining balance
payday_loans.notes: Regulated under Oklahoma Deferred Deposit Lending Act (Okla. Stat. tit. 59, § 3101 et seq.). Borrowers limited to two concurrent payday loans. Lenders required to report to state deferred deposit lending database. Maximum loan term is 12-45 days. Finance charges calculated using tiered fee structure.

title_loans.notes: Motor vehicle title loans are not explicitly authorized under Oklahoma law and are effectively prohibited. State does not have specific title lending statute.

installment_loans.rate_cap: Governed by Oklahoma Consumer Finance Act (Okla. Stat. tit. 59, § 1501 et seq.) and general usury cap of 6% unless parties agree otherwise in writing
installment_loans.notes: Consumer finance companies licensed by Department of Consumer Credit may charge rates up to limits specified in loan agreement, subject to Oklahoma Consumer Finance Act requirements. Finance charges must be disclosed clearly.

mortgage.notes: Oklahoma mortgages regulated under state common law and statutory provisions (Okla. Stat. tit. 109). Oklahoma permits both judicial and non-judicial foreclosures. Non-judicial foreclosure requires power of sale clause in mortgage. Judicial foreclosure available in district courts. Redemption period of 18 months applies post-foreclosure. Residential mortgage lenders must be licensed; licensing administered by Oklahoma Department of Consumer Credit. Reverse mortgages available for seniors age 62+.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (36 U.S.C. § 987) applies in Oklahoma and caps the annual percentage rate (MAPR) at 36% for active-duty service members and their families. This cap applies to payday loans, title loans, installment loans, and other consumer credit extended to covered borrowers. The Department of Defense enforces MLA provisions; covered borrowers may file complaints with DoD.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 U.S.C. § 3953) provides covered service members with a 6% cap on interest accruing on pre-service debts, eviction/foreclosure protections requiring court proceedings, and lease termination rights for military-related moves. SCRA protections apply automatically upon deployment or service; borrowers should provide military documentation to creditors.
state_specific_protections: Oklahoma provides property tax exemptions for veterans with service-connected disabilities rated at 10% or higher by the VA (Okla. Stat. tit. 11, § 22-105)
Oklahoma Home Loan Guarantee Board offers state-backed mortgage assistance and down payment programs for eligible Oklahoma veterans
Oklahoma Military Department administers veteran benefits and can provide resources regarding creditor protections and debt relief programs
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: Oklahoma allows VA-guaranteed loans for home purchases with no down payment requirement. VA loans are exempt from certain Oklahoma closing costs. Property tax homestead exemptions available for qualifying veterans. Oklahoma Housing Finance Agency provides information on VA loan programs and state veteran housing initiatives.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Tinker Air Force Base (Oklahoma City); Fort Washita historic military site. Tinker AFB is a major installation with significant active-duty, reserve, and civilian workforce relevant to MLA enforcement.

complaint_resources.name: Oklahoma Department of Consumer Credit
complaint_resources.description: Licenses and regulates payday lenders, credit repair companies, consumer finance companies, and mortgage lenders. Handles complaints regarding payday loans, deferred deposit lending, credit services, and other consumer credit products.

complaint_resources.name: Oklahoma Attorney General Consumer Protection Unit
complaint_resources.description: Enforces consumer protection laws, investigates fraud and deceptive practices in lending and credit services, and handles consumer complaints. Can pursue actions against predatory lenders and credit repair scams.

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints about credit repair fraud, predatory lending, and other unfair or deceptive consumer financial practices. Maintains national database of complaints.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints about payday loans, personal loans, mortgages, credit reporting, and other consumer financial products. Forwards complaints to relevant regulators.

complaint_resources.name: Oklahoma Deferred Deposit Lending Board
complaint_resources.description: Oversees compliance with payday lending regulations and manages state database of deferred deposit loans to prevent loan stacking and violations.

statute_links.name: Oklahoma Deferred Deposit Lending Act
statute_links.description: Primary statute regulating payday loans in Oklahoma (Okla. Stat. tit. 59, § 3101 et seq.). Establishes loan caps, fee structures, term limits, and borrower restrictions.

statute_links.name: Oklahoma Credit Services Organization Act
statute_links.description: Regulates credit repair companies and requires bonding, registration, written contracts, and prohibits upfront fees (Okla. Stat. tit. 59, § 1501 et seq.).

statute_links.name: Oklahoma Consumer Finance Act
statute_links.description: Governs consumer finance companies and installment loans, including licensing and rate disclosure requirements (Okla. Stat. tit. 59).

statute_links.name: Oklahoma Usury Law
statute_links.description: Establishes default 6% annual interest rate for loans lacking specified interest rate agreement (Okla. Stat. tit. 15, § 266).

statute_links.name: Oklahoma Real Property Mortgage Foreclosure Law
statute_links.description: Governs mortgage foreclosure procedures, redemption rights, and lender licensing (Okla. Stat. tit. 109).

statute_links.name: Oklahoma Department of Consumer Credit Rules and Regulations
statute_links.description: Administrative rules implementing consumer credit statutes, available through Oklahoma Secretary of State and Department of Consumer Credit websites.

OK.recent_changes: No significant changes to Oklahoma consumer lending or credit repair legislation in 2024-2025. Payday lending regulations, usury caps, and credit repair company requirements remain consistent with existing statutory framework established in prior legislative sessions.

OR.name: Oregon
OR.usury_cap: 36% APR cap on payday loans (2007 reform); 12% general usury
key_regulations: Payday loans capped at 36% APR including fees since 2007 reform.
Maximum payday loan is $50,000 or 25% of gross monthly income.
Minimum 31-day term required for payday loans.
The Oregon Consumer Finance Act provides comprehensive lending regulations.
OR.consumer_rights_summary: Oregon capped payday loan APR at 36% in 2007, drastically reducing high-cost lending in the state. The minimum 31-day term and APR cap make traditional payday lending impractical. Consumers can file complaints with the Division of Financial Regulation or the Attorney General.

key_provisions: Credit repair organizations must provide written contract before any services are rendered, clearly disclosing all terms, conditions, and costs
Prohibited from charging upfront fees before services are delivered; payment only allowed after promised results are achieved
Must disclose consumer's right to dispute negative information directly with credit reporting agencies at no cost
Required to provide written disclosure of consumer's right to obtain free credit report from each of the three major credit bureaus
Cannot make misleading or false claims about ability to improve credit score or remove accurate negative information
Must maintain client trust account separate from operating funds and comply with bonding requirements
credit_repair_laws.registration_agency: Oregon Division of Financial Regulation

personal_loans.rate_cap: 12% APR general usury cap (Or. Rev. Stat. § 82.010), with exceptions for regulated lenders
personal_loans.notes: Subject to Oregon Consumer Finance Act (Or. Rev. Stat. § 83.010 et seq.). Licensed lenders may charge higher rates if in compliance with specific lending regulations. Unlicensed lenders bound by 12% cap.

payday_loans.rate_cap: 36% APR including all fees (Or. Rev. Stat. § 86.148)
payday_loans.max_amount: $50,000 or 25% of gross monthly income, whichever is less
payday_loans.notes: 2007 reform established 36% APR cap, making traditional payday lending largely impractical in Oregon. Minimum 31-day term required. Lenders must participate in Oregon Payday Loan Database (OPLD). Borrower can have only one outstanding payday loan at a time. One-day waiting period required between loans.

title_loans.notes: Oregon prohibits vehicle title lending. Loans secured by vehicle titles are not permitted under state law.

installment_loans.rate_cap: Governed by Oregon Consumer Finance Act; rates vary based on loan amount and lender licensing. Licensed consumer finance lenders subject to Or. Rev. Stat. § 83.010 et seq.
installment_loans.notes: Installment loans regulated under Oregon Consumer Finance Act. Maximum rates and terms depend on specific loan classification and lender type. Finance charges must be clearly disclosed.

mortgage.foreclosure_type: Non-judicial foreclosure permitted; judicial foreclosure also available
mortgage.key_regulations: Oregon mortgages governed by Or. Rev. Stat. § 86.705 et seq. (Residential Mortgage Lending Act). Lenders must be licensed through Oregon Division of Financial Regulation. Non-judicial foreclosure requires compliance with statutory notice requirements (Or. Rev. Stat. § 86.745). Pre-foreclosure mediation available in certain circumstances. Right to cure period of at least 120 days required before foreclosure sale.
mortgage.notes: Oregon allows non-judicial foreclosure via trustee's sale with proper notice and statutory compliance. Judicial foreclosure also available. Borrowers have right to cure through final bid date. Deficiency judgments permitted but limited by anti-deficiency protections in some circumstances.

veteran_protections.federal_mla_summary: The Military Lending Act (36 USCA § 3953) imposes a 36% Military Annual Percentage Rate (MAPR) cap on covered borrowings for active duty service members and their dependents. This applies to payday loans, vehicle title loans, tax refund anticipation loans, and similar high-cost credit products. Oregon's payday loan 36% APR cap aligns with federal MLA requirements.
veteran_protections.federal_scra_summary: The Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (50 USCA § 3901 et seq.) provides active duty military members with: 6% interest rate cap on pre-service debts, protection from default judgments and foreclosures during active duty, right to terminate certain contracts and leases, and eviction protections. Creditors must verify military status and apply SCRA protections upon request.
state_specific_protections: Oregon allows service members to file complaints with the Oregon Attorney General Consumer Protection Division regarding predatory lending
Oregon Division of Financial Regulation actively enforces lending laws against unlicensed lenders targeting military personnel
Oregon recognizes Federal Home Loan Bank of Seattle programs supporting military families with down payment assistance and favorable mortgage terms
State law provides that service members on active duty may suspend certain business obligations without penalty during military service
veteran_protections.va_loan_notes: Oregon participates in VA loan programs through private lenders and mortgage companies licensed by the state. No state-specific VA loan interest rate cap beyond federal requirements. Oregon offers property tax exemptions for disabled veterans (or surviving spouses) with service-connected disabilities rated at 10% or higher (Or. Rev. Stat. § 307.381). Veterans' home loan program administered through Oregon Department of Veterans' Affairs.
veteran_protections.military_installations: Major installations: Naval Base Kitsap-Bangor (WA, nearby); Naval Air Station Kingsville (TX); Umatilla Chemical Depot (OR - closed); significant military presence in Portland area. MLA enforcement focus on lenders operating near Joint Base Lewis-McChord (WA, nearby) and recruiting stations throughout Oregon.

complaint_resources.name: Oregon Division of Financial Regulation
complaint_resources.description: Handles licensing and complaints against mortgage lenders, credit service organizations, payday lenders, and other financial service providers. Enforces Oregon Consumer Finance Act and Payday Loan regulations.

complaint_resources.name: Oregon Attorney General - Consumer Protection Division
complaint_resources.description: Investigates consumer complaints regarding unfair/deceptive practices, predatory lending, credit repair fraud, and other consumer finance violations. Can file complaints online.

complaint_resources.name: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB)
complaint_resources.description: Accepts complaints about payday lenders, mortgage servicers, credit reporting agencies, debt collectors, and other consumer financial service providers operating nationwide.

complaint_resources.name: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
complaint_resources.description: Handles complaints about credit repair fraud, identity theft, debt collection abuse, and unfair lending practices. Can file identity theft reports and complaints online.

complaint_resources.name: Better Business Bureau - Oregon
complaint_resources.description: Provides consumer complaint resolution and business ratings for lenders and credit service organizations operating in Oregon.

statute_links.name: Oregon Consumer Finance Act (Or. Rev. Stat. § 83.010 et seq.)
statute_links.description: Comprehensive regulation of consumer lending in Oregon, including installment loans, license requirements, rate caps, and disclosure requirements.

statute_links.name: Oregon Payday Loan Law (Or. Rev. Stat. § 86.148)
statute_links.description: Establishes 36% APR cap on payday loans, 31-day minimum term, and participation in Oregon Payday Loan Database (OPLD).

statute_links.name: Oregon Credit Services Organization Act (Or. Rev. Stat. § 646.601-646.651)
statute_links.description: Regulates credit repair companies, requires licensing, prohibits upfront fees, establishes bond requirements, and provides consumer cancellation rights.

statute_links.name: Oregon Residential Mortgage Lending Act (Or. Rev. Stat. § 86.705 et seq.)
statute_links.description: Governs mortgage lending, lender licensing, foreclosure procedures, and non-judicial foreclosure requirements.

statute_links.name: Oregon Usury Statutes (Or. Rev. Stat. § 82.010)
statute_links.description: Establishes 12% general usury cap and exceptions for regulated lenders, credit unions, and other financial institutions.

statute_links.name: Oregon Payday Loan Database (OPLD) Rules (Or. Admin. R. 161-098-0000 et seq.)
statute_links.description: Rules governing participation in statewide database to prevent multiple outstanding payday loans and enforce one-loan-at-a-time restriction.

OR.recent_changes: No significant changes to Oregon's consumer lending or credit repair laws in 2024-2025. The 2007 payday loan reform (36% APR cap) remains the most substantial recent legislative action affecting high-cost lending in Oregon.

PA.name: Pennsylvania
PA.usury_cap: 6% for non-licensed lenders (24% for licensed small loan companies); payday lending banned
PA.consumer_protection_agency: Pennsylvania Attorney General Bureau of Consumer Protection
PA.licensing_board: Pennsylvania Department of Banking and Securities
key_regulations: Payday lending is banned; the state's usury cap of 6% (24% for licensed lenders) prevents it.
Licensed consumer discount companies regulated under the Consumer Discount Company Act.
The Pennsylvania Unfair Trade Practices and Consumer Protection Law prohibits deceptive lending.
